				\item Si $j$ está marcado $i$ no está marcado e $(i,j)$ es verde o rojo: marcar $i$.
			\end{itemize}
			\item Repetir el proceso hasta marcar el máximo posible de vértices.
		\end{itemize}
		\item Si al final el vértice $t$ resulta marcado, $(t,s)$ pertenece a un ciclo por el que es posible incrementar el flujo de $s$ a $t$.
		\item Pero, dado que $f_0$ es máximo, este ciclo no puede existir y, por el lema de coloración de arcos, $(t,s)$ necesariamente  pertenece al cociclo asociado a los vértices marcados.
	\end{itemize}
\end{frame}


\begin{frame}{Flujo máximo y corte de capacidad mínima}
	\begin{itemize}\itemsep 4mm
		\item El cociclo define un corte. 
		\item La capacidad de este corte viene determinada por la capacidad de los arcos verdes del mismo.
		\item Falta por ver que el flujo que pasa de $s$ a $t$ a través de ese corte es igual a la suma de las capacidades de los arcos verdes. 
		\item Por la ley de conservación de flujo:
		$$
		\sum_{u\in w^+(N)} f_u = \sum_{u\in w^-(N)} f_u = f_0 + \sum_{\substack{u\in w^-(N) \\ u\neq (t,s)}} f_u
		$$
		\item Como los arcos verdes están a máxima capacidad y los negros a 0, se concluye que:
		$$
		\sum_{u\in w^+(N)} c_u  = \sum_{u\in w^+(N)} f_u = f_0 
		$$
	\end{itemize}
\end{frame}


\begin{frame}{Flujo máximo y corte de capacidad mínima}
	
	\alert{¿Qué ocurre en este ejemplo?}
	
	\begin{center}
		\begin{tikzpicture}[node_style/.style={draw,circle,minimum size=0.5cm,inner sep=1}]
			\node[node_style] (s) at (0,1.75) {s} ;
			\node[node_style] (1) at (2,3.25) {1} ;
			\node[node_style] (2) at (6,3.25) {2} ;
			\node[node_style] (3) at (2,0.25) {3} ;
			\node[node_style] (4) at (6,0.25) {4} ;
			\node[node_style] (t) at (8,1.75) {t} ;
			
			\draw [line width=1.5pt, color=red] (s) edge[->] node[left]  {\textcolor{red}{5},\textcolor{blue}{(6)}} (1) ;
			\draw [line width=1.5pt, color=red] (s) edge[->] node[left]  {\textcolor{red}{5},\textcolor{blue}{(7)}} (3) ;
			\draw [line width=1.5pt] (3) edge[->] node[right] {\textcolor{red}{0},\textcolor{blue}{(5)}} (1) ;
			\draw [line width=1.5pt, color=red] (1) edge[->] node[right] {\textcolor{red}{1},\textcolor{blue}{(6)}} (4) ;
			\draw [line width=1.5pt, color=green] (1) edge[->] node[above] {\textcolor{red}{4},\textcolor{blue}{(4)}} (2) ;
			\draw [line width=1.5pt, color=green] (3) edge[->] node[below] {\textcolor{red}{5},\textcolor{blue}{(5)}} (4) ;
			\draw[line width=1.5pt, color=green]  (4) edge[->] node[right] {\textcolor{red}{2},\textcolor{blue}{(2)}} (2) ;
			\draw [line width=1.5pt, color=red] (2) edge[->] node[right] {\textcolor{red}{6},\textcolor{blue}{(7)}} (t) ;
			\draw [line width=1.5pt, color=red] (4) edge[->] node[right] {\textcolor{red}{4},\textcolor{blue}{(6)}} (t) ;
			\draw  [line width=1.5pt] (t) edge[->,bend left=100] node[below] {10,\textcolor{blue}{($\infty$)}} (s) ;
		\end{tikzpicture}
	\end{center}
\end{frame}

\begin{frame}{Algoritmo de Ford-Fulkerson I}
	
	Se public\'o en 1956 en \cite{fordfulkerson1956}.
	
	\begin{figure}
		\centering
		\subfloat[L.R. Ford, 1927-2017]{\makebox[.18\textwidth]{\includegraphics[width=0.15\textwidth]{fig/ford}}} \hspace{0.25cm}
		\subfloat[D.R. Fulkerson, 1924-1976]{\makebox[.18\textwidth]{\includegraphics[width=0.15\textwidth]{fig/fulkerson}}}
	\end{figure}
	
	\pause
	
	Se conoce tambi\'en como algoritmo del camino de aumento, ya que la idea principal consiste en aumentar la capacidad de los flujos en los caminos del origen al destino.
\end{frame}

\begin{frame}{Algoritmo de Ford-Fulkerson I}
	
	Aplicando la regla de coloreado de arcos anterior y etiquetando/marcando arcos según se indica en el lema de coloreado de arcos, es posible determinar si un flujo es óptimo o no. 
	
	Además, en caso de que no sea óptimo, este procedimiento nos indica un ciclo por el que es posible incrementar el flujo. 
\end{frame}

\begin{frame}{Algoritmo de Ford-Fulkerson I}
	
	
	\begin{center}
		\begin{tikzpicture}[node_style/.style={draw,circle,minimum size=0.5cm,inner sep=1}]
			\node[node_style] (s) at (0,1.75) {s} ;
			\node[node_style] (2) at (2,3.25) {2} ;
			\node[node_style] (4) at (6,3.25) {4} ;
			\node[node_style] (3) at (2,0.25) {3} ;
			\node[node_style] (5) at (6,0.25) {5} ;
			\node[node_style] (t) at (8,1.75) {t} ;
			
			\draw [line width=1.5pt, color=green] (s) edge[->] node[left]  {\textcolor{red}{2}\textcolor{blue}{(2)}} (3) ;
			\draw [line width=1.5pt, color=black] (3) edge[->] node[right] {\textcolor{red}{0}\textcolor{blue}{(1)}} (2) ;
			\draw [line width=1.5pt, color=green] (3) edge[->] node[below] {\textcolor{red}{2}\textcolor{blue}{(2)}} (5) ;
			\draw [line width=1.5pt, color=black] (4) edge[->] node[right] {\textcolor{red}{0}\textcolor{blue}{(1)}} (3) ;
			\draw [line width=1.5pt, color=red] (5) edge[->] node[right] {\textcolor{red}{1}\textcolor{blue}{(2)}} (t) ;
			\draw[line width=1.5pt, color=green]  (5) edge[->] node[right] {\textcolor{red}{1}\textcolor{blue}{(1)}} (4) ;
			\only<1-3>{
				\draw  [line width=1.5pt, color=black] (t) edge[->,bend left=100] node[below] {3\textcolor{blue}{($\infty$)}} (s) ;
			}
			\only<1>{
				\draw [line width=1.5pt, color=red] (s) edge[->] node[left]  {\textcolor{red}{1}\textcolor{blue}{(3)}} (2) ;
				\draw [line width=1.5pt, color=red] (2) edge[->] node[above] {\textcolor{red}{1}\textcolor{blue}{(2)}} (4) ;
				\draw [line width=1.5pt, color=red] (4) edge[->] node[right] {\textcolor{red}{2}\textcolor{blue}{(3)}} (t) ;
			}
			\only<2-3>{
				\draw [line width=3pt, color=orange] (s) edge[->] node[left]  {\textcolor{red}{1}\textcolor{blue}{(3)}} (2) ;
				\draw [line width=3pt, color=orange] (2) edge[->] node[above] {\textcolor{red}{1}\textcolor{blue}{(2)}} (4) ;
				\draw [line width=3pt, color=orange] (4) edge[->] node[right] {\textcolor{red}{2}\textcolor{blue}{(3)}} (t) ;
			}
			\only<4-5>{
				\draw [line width=1.5pt, color=red] (s) edge[->] node[left]  {\textcolor{red}{2}\textcolor{blue}{(3)}} (2) ;
				\draw [line width=1.5pt, color=green] (2) edge[->] node[above] {\textcolor{red}{2}\textcolor{blue}{(2)}} (4) ;
				\draw [line width=1.5pt, color=green] (4) edge[->] node[right] {\textcolor{red}{3}\textcolor{blue}{(3)}} (t) ;
				\draw  [line width=1.5pt, color=black] (t) edge[->,bend left=100] node[below] {4\textcolor{blue}{($\infty$)}} (s); 
			}
		\end{tikzpicture}
		
		\only<3>{Es posible incrementar una unidad por el camino de arcos naranjas}
		\only<5>{Ya no es posible marcar $t$}
	\end{center}
\end{frame}


\begin{frame}{Algoritmo de Ford-Fulkerson I}
	
	\begin{block}{Definición}
		Planteado un problema de flujo m\'{a}ximo de $s$ a $t$ en la red de transporte $\bigl\{G=(V,U),\vc\bigl\}$, y dado un flujo compatible $\vf$, se define el \underline{grafo incremental} $G(\vf)$ al digrafo $G(\vf) = (V,U({\bf 	f}))$, en el que el conjunto de arcos $U(\vf)$ se construye de la
		siguiente forma: 
		$$ 
		\forall u =(i,j) \in U \left\{ \begin{array}{lrr} u^+=(i,j) \in U(\vf) & si & f_u < c_u \\[3mm] 	u^-=(j,i) \in U(\vf) & si & f_u > 0 \end{array} \right. 
		$$
	\end{block}
	\footnotesize
	\begin{itemize}
		\item Se añade un arco al grafo incremental en el mismo sentido que el arco de la red cuando el flujo del arco en la red se puede incrementar. Se le asocia un peso $\ell_u=c_u - f_u$
		\item Se añade un arco al grafo incremental en sentiod contrario al del arco de la red, cuando el flujo del arco en la red se puede reducir. Se le asocia un peso $\ell_u=f_u$
		\item Estas operaciones no son excluyentes. 
		\item El arco de retorno no se incluye en el grafo incremental.
		\item A cada arco de $U({\bf f})$ se le asocia una cantidad que representa el flujo que admite el arco en cuesti\'{o}n: cuando el arco permita incremento de flujo y $f_u$ cuando permita la disminuci\'{o}n del mismo.
	\end{itemize}
	
\end{frame}

\begin{frame}{Algoritmo de Ford-Fulkerson I}
	
	Construir el red asociada a la siguiente red de transporte:
	\begin{center}
		\begin{tikzpicture}[node_style/.style={draw,circle,minimum size=0.5cm,inner sep=1}]
			\node[node_style] (s) at (0,1.75) {s} ;
			\node[node_style] (2) at (2,3.25) {2} ;
			\node[node_style] (4) at (6,3.25) {4} ;
			\node[node_style] (3) at (2,0.25) {3} ;
			\node[node_style] (5) at (6,0.25) {5} ;
			\node[node_style] (t) at (8,1.75) {t} ;
			\only<1>{
				\draw [line width=1.15pt, color=green] (s) edge[->] node[left]  {\textcolor{red}{2}\textcolor{blue}{(2)}} (3) ;
				\draw [line width=1.15pt, color=black] (3) edge[->] node[right] {\textcolor{red}{0}\textcolor{blue}{(1)}} (2) ;
				\draw [line width=1.15pt, color=green] (3) edge[->] node[below] {\textcolor{red}{2}\textcolor{blue}{(2)}} (5) ;
				\draw [line width=1.15pt, color=black] (4) edge[->] node[right] {\textcolor{red}{0}\textcolor{blue}{(1)}} (3) ;
				\draw [line width=1.15pt, color=red] (5) edge[->] node[right] {\textcolor{red}{1}\textcolor{blue}{(2)}} (t) ;
				\draw[line width=1.15pt, color=green]  (5) edge[->] node[right] {\textcolor{red}{1}\textcolor{blue}{(1)}} (4) ;
				\draw  [line width=1.15pt, color=black] (t) edge[->,bend left=100] node[below] {3\textcolor{blue}{($\infty$)}} (s) ;
				\draw [line width=1.15pt, color=red] (s) edge[->] node[left]  {\textcolor{red}{1}\textcolor{blue}{(3)}} (2) ;
				\draw [line width=1.15pt, color=red] (2) edge[->] node[above] {\textcolor{red}{1}\textcolor{blue}{(2)}} (4) ;
				\draw [line width=1.15pt, color=red] (4) edge[->] node[right] {\textcolor{red}{2}\textcolor{blue}{(3)}} (t) ;
			}
			\only<2>{
				\draw [line width=1.15pt] (s) edge[<-] node[left]  {2} (3) ;
				\draw [line width=1.15pt] (3) edge[->] node[right] {1} (2) ;
				\draw [line width=1.15pt] (3) edge[<-] node[below] {2} (5) ;
				\draw [line width=1.15pt] (4) edge[->] node[right] {1} (3) ;
				\draw [line width=1.15pt] (5) edge[->,bend left=8] node[above] {1} (t) ;
				\draw [line width=1.15pt] (5) edge[<-,bend right=8] node[below] {1} (t) ;
				\draw[line width=1.15pt]  (5) edge[<-] node[right] {1} (4) ;
				%		\draw  [line width=1.5pt] (t) edge[->,bend left=100] node[below] {3\textcolor{blue}{($\infty$)}} (s) ;
				\draw [line width=1.15pt] (s) edge[->,bend left=8] node[above]  {2} (2) ;
				\draw [line width=1.15pt] (s) edge[<-,bend right=8] node[below]  {1} (2) ;
				\draw [line width=1.15pt] (2) edge[->,bend left=8] node[above] {1} (4) ;
				\draw [line width=1.15pt] (2) edge[<-,bend right=8] node[below] {1} (4) ;
				\draw [line width=1.15pt] (4) edge[->,bend left=8] node[above] {1} (t) ;
				\draw [line width=1.15pt] (4) edge[<-,bend right=8] node[below] {2} (t) ;
			}
		\end{tikzpicture}
		
	\end{center}
\end{frame}



\begin{frame}{Algoritmo de Ford-Fulkerson I}
	\begin{block}{Clave}
		La clave del algoritmo radica en que el flujo ${\bf f}$ permite un incremento de flujo en el valor del flujo $f_0$ si, y s\'{o}lo si, existe un camino $\mu[s,t]$ en el grafo incremental $G({\bf f})$. 
	\end{block}
\end{frame}

\begin{frame}{Algoritmo de Ford-Fulkerson I}
	\begin{enumerate}[\mbox{Paso} 1]\itemsep 2mm
		\item $k=0$. Determinar un flujo inicial $f^0=(f_0^0,f_1^0,\dots,f_m^0)$.
		\item Definir la red incremental $G(\vf^k)=(V,U(\vf^k))$. 
		
		Buscar un camino $\mu^k$ de $s$ a $t$ en $G(\vf^k)$.
		\begin{itemize}
			\item Si no existe el camino: PARAR, $\vf^k$ es óptimo.
			\item En caso contrario, ir al paso siguiente.
		\end{itemize}
		\item Sea $\epsilon^k=\min\{\ell_u\tq u\in\mu^k,\, u\in U(\vf^k)\}$
		
		Para cada arco $u\in U$ de la red de transporte:
		$$
		f_u^{k+1} = \begin{cases}
			f_u^{k+1} = f_u^k +\epsilon^k & \text{ si } u\in\mu^k(+) \\
			f_u^{k+1} = f_u^k -\epsilon^k & \text{ si } u\in\mu^k(-) \\
			f_u^{k+1} = f_u^k  & \text{ si } a\notin\mu^k(+)\cup\{(t,s)\} \\
			f_u^{k+1} = f_u^k +\epsilon^k  & \text{ si } u=(t,s)\\
		\end{cases}
		$$
		Hacer $k=k+1$ e ir al paso 2.
	\end{enumerate}
\end{frame}
\begin{frame}{Algoritmo de Ford-Fulkerson I}
	
	\only<1>{Aplicar el algoritmo anterior a esta red de transporte}
	\only<2>{Paso 1. $k=0$. $f_0=(0,0,\dots,0)$}
	\only<3>{Paso 2. Red incremental}
	\only<4>{Paso 2. Camino $(s-2-4-t)$. $\epsilon=\min\{3,2,3\}=2$}
	\only<5>{Paso 3. Se actualiza el flujo por el camino}
	\only<6>{Paso 2. Red incremental}
	\only<7>{Paso 2. Camino $(s-3-5-t)$. $\epsilon=\min\{2,2,2\}=2$}
	\only<8>{Paso 3. Se actualiza el flujo por el camino}
	\only<9>{Paso 2. Red incremental. No existe camino. Flujo óptimo}
	\visible<10>{Único vértice alcanzable: 2. $N=\{s,2\}$: corte capacidad mínima}
	\begin{center}
		\begin{tikzpicture}[node_style/.style={draw,circle,minimum size=0.5cm,inner sep=1}]
			\only<1-9>{
				\node[node_style] (s) at (0,1.75) {s} ;
				\node[node_style] (2) at (2,3.25) {2} ;
			}
			\only<10>{
				\node[node_style,color=limegreen] (s) at (0,1.75) {s} ;
				\node[node_style,color=limegreen] (2) at (2,3.25) {2} ;
			}
			\node[node_style] (4) at (6,3.25) {4} ;
			\node[node_style] (3) at (2,0.25) {3} ;
			\node[node_style] (5) at (6,0.25) {5} ;
			\node[node_style] (t) at (8,1.75) {t} ;
			\only<1>{
				\draw [line width=1.5pt] (s) edge[->] node[left]  {\textcolor{red}{}\textcolor{blue}{(2)}} (3) ;
				\draw [line width=1.5pt] (3) edge[->] node[right] {\textcolor{red}{}\textcolor{blue}{(1)}} (2) ;
				\draw [line width=1.5pt] (3) edge[->] node[below] {\textcolor{red}{}\textcolor{blue}{(2)}} (5) ;
				\draw [line width=1.5pt] (4) edge[->] node[right] {\textcolor{red}{}\textcolor{blue}{(1)}} (3) ;
				\draw [line width=1.5pt] (5) edge[->] node[right] {\textcolor{red}{}\textcolor{blue}{(2)}} (t) ;
				\draw[line width=1.5pt]  (5) edge[->] node[right] {\textcolor{red}{}\textcolor{blue}{(1)}} (4) ;
				\draw  [line width=1.5pt] (t) edge[->,bend left=100] node[below] {\textcolor{blue}{($\infty$)}} (s) ;
				\draw [line width=1.5pt] (s) edge[->] node[left]  {\textcolor{red}{}\textcolor{blue}{(3)}} (2) ;
				\draw [line width=1.5pt] (2) edge[->] node[above] {\textcolor{red}{}\textcolor{blue}{(2)}} (4) ;
				\draw [line width=1.5pt] (4) edge[->] node[right] {\textcolor{red}{}\textcolor{blue}{(3)}} (t) ;
			}
			\only<2>{
				\draw [line width=1.5pt, color=black] (s) edge[->] node[left]  {\textcolor{red}{0}\textcolor{blue}{(2)}} (3) ;
				\draw [line width=1.5pt, color=black] (3) edge[->] node[right] {\textcolor{red}{0}\textcolor{blue}{(1)}} (2) ;
				\draw [line width=1.5pt, color=black] (3) edge[->] node[below] {\textcolor{red}{0}\textcolor{blue}{(2)}} (5) ;
				\draw [line width=1.5pt, color=black] (4) edge[->] node[right] {\textcolor{red}{0}\textcolor{blue}{(1)}} (3) ;
				\draw [line width=1.5pt, color=black] (5) edge[->] node[right] {\textcolor{red}{0}\textcolor{blue}{(2)}} (t) ;
				\draw[line width=1.5pt, color=black]  (5) edge[->] node[right] {\textcolor{red}{0}\textcolor{blue}{(1)}} (4) ;
				\draw  [line width=1.5pt, color=black] (t) edge[->,bend left=100] node[below] {0\textcolor{blue}{($\infty$)}} (s) ;
				\draw [line width=1.5pt, color=black] (s) edge[->] node[left]  {\textcolor{red}{0}\textcolor{blue}{(3)}} (2) ;
				\draw [line width=1.5pt, color=black] (2) edge[->] node[above] {\textcolor{red}{0}\textcolor{blue}{(2)}} (4) ;
				\draw [line width=1.5pt, color=black] (4) edge[->] node[right] {\textcolor{red}{0}\textcolor{blue}{(3)}} (t) ;
			}
			\only<3>{
				\draw [line width=1.5pt] (s) edge[->] node[left]  {2} (3) ;
				\draw [line width=1.5pt] (3) edge[->] node[right] {1} (2) ;
				\draw [line width=1.5pt] (3) edge[->] node[below] {2} (5) ;
				\draw [line width=1.5pt] (4) edge[->] node[right] {1} (3) ;
				\draw [line width=1.5pt] (5) edge[->] node[right] {2} (t) ;
				\draw[line width=1.5pt]  (5) edge[->] node[right] {1} (4) ;
				%	\draw  [line width=1.5pt, color=black] (t) edge[->,bend left=100] node[below] {0\textcolor{blue}{($\infty$)}} (s) ;
				\draw [line width=1.5pt] (s) edge[->] node[left]  {3} (2) ;
				\draw [line width=1.5pt] (2) edge[->] node[above] {2} (4) ;
				\draw [line width=1.5pt] (4) edge[->] node[right] {3} (t) ;
			}
			\only<4>{
				\draw [line width=1.5pt] (s) edge[->] node[left]  {2} (3) ;
				\draw [line width=1.5pt] (3) edge[->] node[right] {1} (2) ;
				\draw [line width=1.5pt] (3) edge[->] node[below] {2} (5) ;
				\draw [line width=1.5pt] (4) edge[->] node[right] {1} (3) ;
				\draw [line width=1.5pt] (5) edge[->] node[right] {2} (t) ;
				\draw[line width=1.5pt]  (5) edge[->] node[right] {1} (4) ;
				%	\draw  [line width=1.5pt, color=black] (t) edge[->,bend left=100] node[below] {0\textcolor{blue}{($\infty$)}} (s) ;
				\draw [line width=2.5pt] (s) edge[->] node[left]  {3} (2) ;
				\draw [line width=2.5pt] (2) edge[->] node[above] {2} (4) ;
				\draw [line width=2.5pt] (4) edge[->] node[right] {3} (t) ;
			}
			\only<5>{
				\draw [line width=1.5pt, color=black] (s) edge[->] node[left]  {\textcolor{red}{0}\textcolor{blue}{(2)}} (3) ;
				\draw [line width=1.5pt, color=black] (3) edge[->] node[right] {\textcolor{red}{0}\textcolor{blue}{(1)}} (2) ;
				\draw [line width=1.5pt, color=black] (3) edge[->] node[below] {\textcolor{red}{0}\textcolor{blue}{(2)}} (5) ;
				\draw [line width=1.5pt, color=black] (4) edge[->] node[right] {\textcolor{red}{0}\textcolor{blue}{(1)}} (3) ;
				\draw [line width=1.5pt, color=black] (5) edge[->] node[right] {\textcolor{red}{0}\textcolor{blue}{(2)}} (t) ;
				\draw[line width=1.5pt, color=black]  (5) edge[->] node[right] {\textcolor{red}{0}\textcolor{blue}{(1)}} (4) ;
				\draw  [line width=1.5pt, color=black] (t) edge[->,bend left=100] node[below] {2\textcolor{blue}{($\infty$)}} (s) ;
				\draw [line width=1.5pt, color=red] (s) edge[->] node[left]  {\textcolor{red}{2}\textcolor{blue}{(3)}} (2) ;
				\draw [line width=1.5pt, color=green] (2) edge[->] node[above] {\textcolor{red}{2}\textcolor{blue}{(2)}} (4) ;
				\draw [line width=1.5pt, color=red] (4) edge[->] node[right] {\textcolor{red}{2}\textcolor{blue}{(3)}} (t) ;
			}
			\only<6>{
				\draw [line width=1.5pt] (s) edge[->] node[left]  {2} (3) ;
				\draw [line width=1.5pt] (3) edge[->] node[right] {1} (2) ;
				\draw [line width=1.5pt] (3) edge[->] node[below] {2} (5) ;
				\draw [line width=1.5pt] (4) edge[->] node[right] {1} (3) ;
				\draw [line width=1.5pt] (5) edge[->] node[right] {2} (t) ;
				\draw[line width=1.5pt]  (5) edge[->] node[right] {1} (4) ;
				%	\draw  [line width=1.5pt, color=black] (t) edge[->,bend left=100] node[below] {0\textcolor{blue}{($\infty$)}} (s) ;
				\draw [line width=1.5pt] (s) edge[->,bend left=8] node[above]  {1} (2) ;
				\draw [line width=1.5pt] (s) edge[<-,bend right=8] node[below]  {2} (2) ;
				\draw [line width=1.5pt] (2) edge[<-] node[above] {2} (4) ;
				\draw [line width=1.5pt] (4) edge[->,bend left=8] node[above] {1} (t) ;
				\draw [line width=1.5pt] (4) edge[<-,bend right=8] node[below] {2} (t) ;
			}
			\only<7>{
				\draw [line width=2.5pt] (s) edge[->] node[left]  {2} (3) ;
				\draw [line width=1.5pt] (3) edge[->] node[right] {1} (2) ;
				\draw [line width=2.5pt] (3) edge[->] node[below] {2} (5) ;
				\draw [line width=1.5pt] (4) edge[->] node[right] {1} (3) ;
				\draw [line width=2.5pt] (5) edge[->] node[right] {2} (t) ;
				\draw[line width=1.5pt]  (5) edge[->] node[right] {1} (4) ;
				%	\draw  [line width=1.5pt, color=black] (t) edge[->,bend left=100] node[below] {0\textcolor{blue}{($\infty$)}} (s) ;
				\draw [line width=1.5pt] (s) edge[->,bend left=8] node[above]  {1} (2) ;
				\draw [line width=1.5pt] (s) edge[<-,bend right=8] node[below]  {2} (2) ;
				\draw [line width=1.5pt] (2) edge[<-] node[above] {2} (4) ;
				\draw [line width=1.5pt] (4) edge[->,bend left=8] node[above] {1} (t) ;
				\draw [line width=1.5pt] (4) edge[<-,bend right=8] node[below] {2} (t) ;
			}
			\only<8>{
				\draw [line width=1.5pt, color=green] (s) edge[->] node[left]  {\textcolor{red}{2}\textcolor{blue}{(2)}} (3) ;
				\draw [line width=1.5pt, color=black] (3) edge[->] node[right] {\textcolor{red}{0}\textcolor{blue}{(1)}} (2) ;
				\draw [line width=1.5pt, color=green] (3) edge[->] node[below] {\textcolor{red}{2}\textcolor{blue}{(2)}} (5) ;
				\draw [line width=1.5pt, color=black] (4) edge[->] node[right] {\textcolor{red}{0}\textcolor{blue}{(1)}} (3) ;
				\draw [line width=1.5pt, color=green] (5) edge[->] node[right] {\textcolor{red}{2}\textcolor{blue}{(2)}} (t) ;
				\draw[line width=1.5pt, color=black]  (5) edge[->] node[right] {\textcolor{red}{0}\textcolor{blue}{(1)}} (4) ;
				\draw  [line width=1.5pt, color=black] (t) edge[->,bend left=100] node[below] {4\textcolor{blue}{($\infty$)}} (s) ;
				\draw [line width=1.5pt, color=red] (s) edge[->] node[left]  {\textcolor{red}{2}\textcolor{blue}{(3)}} (2) ;
				\draw [line width=1.5pt, color=green] (2) edge[->] node[above] {\textcolor{red}{2}\textcolor{blue}{(2)}} (4) ;
				\draw [line width=1.5pt, color=red] (4) edge[->] node[right] {\textcolor{red}{2}\textcolor{blue}{(3)}} (t) ;
			}
			\only<9>{
				\draw [line width=1.5pt] (s) edge[<-] node[left]  {2} (3) ;
				\draw [line width=1.5pt] (3) edge[->] node[right] {1} (2) ;
				\draw [line width=1.5pt] (3) edge[<-] node[below] {2} (5) ;
				\draw [line width=1.5pt] (4) edge[->] node[right] {1} (3) ;
				\draw [line width=1.5pt] (5) edge[<-] node[right] {2} (t) ;
				\draw[line width=1.5pt]  (5) edge[->] node[right] {1} (4) ;
				%	\draw  [line width=1.5pt, color=black] (t) edge[->,bend left=100] node[below] {0\textcolor{blue}{($\infty$)}} (s) ;
				\draw [line width=1.5pt] (s) edge[->,bend left=8] node[above]  {1} (2) ;
				\draw [line width=1.5pt] (s) edge[<-,bend right=8] node[below]  {2} (2) ;
				\draw [line width=1.5pt] (2) edge[<-] node[above] {2} (4) ;
				\draw [line width=1.5pt] (4) edge[->,bend left=8] node[above] {1} (t) ;
				\draw [line width=1.5pt] (4) edge[<-,bend right=8] node[below] {2} (t) ;
			}
			\only<10>{
				\draw [line width=1.5pt,color=blue] (s) edge[<-] node[left]  {2} (3) ;
				\draw [line width=1.5pt,color=blue] (3) edge[->] node[right] {1} (2) ;
				\draw [line width=1.5pt] (3) edge[<-] node[below] {2} (5) ;
				\draw [line width=1.5pt] (4) edge[->] node[right] {1} (3) ;
				\draw [line width=1.5pt] (5) edge[<-] node[right] {2} (t) ;
				\draw[line width=1.5pt]  (5) edge[->] node[right] {1} (4) ;
				%	\draw  [line width=1.5pt, color=black] (t) edge[->,bend left=100] node[below] {0\textcolor{blue}{($\infty$)}} (s) ;
				\draw [line width=1.5pt] (s) edge[->,bend left=8] node[above]  {1} (2) ;
				\draw [line width=1.5pt] (s) edge[<-,bend right=8] node[below]  {2} (2) ;
				\draw [line width=1.5pt,color=blue] (2) edge[<-] node[above] {2} (4) ;
				\draw [line width=1.5pt] (4) edge[->,bend left=8] node[above] {1} (t) ;
				\draw [line width=1.5pt] (4) edge[<-,bend right=8] node[below] {2} (t) ;
			}
		\end{tikzpicture}
		\vfill
	\end{center}
\end{frame}


\begin{frame}{Algoritmo de Ford-Fulkerson. Etiquetado de vértices}
	
	\begin{itemize}\itemsep 5mm	
		\item El algoritmo se puede plantear de forma más sencilla, sin necesidad de plantear explícitamente el grafo incremental en cada iteración. 
		\item Para ello, se sigue una regla de etiquetado de vértices.
		\item Inicialmente se asigna una etiqueta al vértice inicial $s$ y, paso a paso se asignan etiquetas al resto de los vértices.
		\item La etiqueta de un vértice se asigna a partir de:
		\begin{itemize}\itemsep 2mm
			\item la etiqueta de un vértice adyacente suyo,
			\item el flujo que circula a través del arco que une estos dos	vértices,
			\item la capacidad de ese arco.
		\end{itemize}
	\end{itemize}
\end{frame}

\begin{frame}{Algoritmo de Ford-Fulkerson. Etiquetado de vértices}
	Las etiquetas tienen dos tipos de información
	\alert{\large
		$$
		(\pm i, \delta_j)
		$$
	}
	\begin{enumerate}\itemsep 5mm
		
		\item La primera parte, {\color{blue} $+i$} ó {\color{blue} $-i$}, indica el nodo que se utiliza para etiquetar el nodo $j$.
		
		$i$ debe ser un nodo ya etiquetado.
		
		\item La segunda parte, {\color{blue} $\delta_j$}, indica el flujo incremental que es posible enviar a $j$ desde $s$ a través del $i$.
	\end{enumerate}
	
\end{frame}

\begin{frame}{Algoritmo de Ford-Fulkerson. Etiquetado de vértices}
	\begin{itemize}
		\item 	Si el arco que une el nodo $i$ que se utiliza para etiquetar el vértice $j$ (no etiquetado) tiene origen en $i$ y final en $j$, se etiqueta con $\color{blue} +i$.
		\begin{center}
			\begin{tikzpicture}[node_style/.style={draw,circle,minimum size=0.5cm,inner sep=1}]
				\node at (0,0.5) {$(k,\delta_i)$} ;
				\node at (3,0.5) {$(+i,\delta_j)$} ;
				\node[node_style] (i) at (0,0) {$i$} ;
				\node[node_style] (j) at (3,0) {$j$} ;
				\draw [line width=1.5pt] (i) edge[->] node[above] {\textcolor{red}{$f_{ij}$}\textcolor{blue}{($c_{ij}$)}} (j) ;
			\end{tikzpicture}
		\end{center}
		
		\item Si el arco que une el nodo $i$ que se utiliza para etiquetar el vértice $j$  (no etiquetado)  tiene origen en $j$ y final en $i$, se etiqueta con $\color{blue} -i$.
		\begin{center}
			\begin{tikzpicture}[node_style/.style={draw,circle,minimum size=0.5cm,inner sep=1}]
				\node at (0,0.5) {$(k,\delta_i)$} ;
				\node at (3,0.5) {$(-i,\delta_j)$} ;
				\node[node_style] (i) at (0,0) {$i$} ;
				\node[node_style] (j) at (3,0) {$j$} ;
				\draw [line width=1.5pt] (i) edge[<-] node[above] {\textcolor{red}{$f_{ji}$}\textcolor{blue}{($c_{ji}$)}} (j) ;
			\end{tikzpicture}
		\end{center}
	\end{itemize}
	Al final, el flujo del arco se puede modificar:
	\begin{itemize}
		\item $+i$:  se incrementa el flujo del arco $(i,j)$ en $\delta_j$ unidades
		\item $-i$:  debe reducirse el flujo del arco $(j,i)$ en $\delta_j$ unidades.
	\end{itemize} 
\end{frame}


\begin{frame}{Algoritmo de Ford-Fulkerson. Etiquetado de vértices}
	\begin{itemize}\itemsep 5mm
		\item Para asignar las etiquetas a los vértices de la red, se empieza
		por la fuente y se va etiquetando en cada iteración un nuevo
		vértice.
		\item Si al final del proceso el vértice sumidero resulta etiquetado, se
		ha encontrado una cadena de arcos por los que incrementar el flujo
		de $s$ a $t$.
		\item Si no es posible etiquetar el sumidero, entonces ya se ha
		encontrado el flujo máximo.
	\end{itemize}
\end{frame}

\begin{frame}{Algoritmo de Ford-Fulkerson. Etiquetado de vértices}{Regla de etiquetado}\small
	
	El vértice $i$ está etiquetado y se quiere etiquetar el vértice $j$.
	\begin{enumerate}\itemsep 0mm
		\item Si existe el arco $(i,j)$ y $\mathbf{f_{ij}<c_{ij}}$, etiquetar $j$ como $(+i,\delta_j)$
		donde
		$
		\delta_j=\min\{\delta_i,c_{ij}-f_{ij}\}
		$
		y representa el flujo que se puede enviar de $s$ a $j$.
		\begin{center}
			\begin{tikzpicture}[node_style/.style={draw,circle,minimum size=0.5cm,inner sep=1}]
				\node at (0,0.5) {$(k,\delta_i)$} ;
				\node at (3,0.5) {$(+i,\delta_j)$} ;
				\node[node_style] (i) at (0,0) {$i$} ;
				\node[node_style] (j) at (3,0) {$j$} ;
				\draw [line width=1.5pt] (i) edge[->] node[above] {\textcolor{red}{$f_{ij}$}\textcolor{blue}{($c_{ij}$)}} (j) ;
			\end{tikzpicture}
		\end{center}
		\item Si existe el arco $(j,i)$ y $\mathbf{0<f_{ij}}$, etiquetar $j$ como $(-i,\delta_j)$
		donde
		$
		\delta_j=\min\{\delta_i,f_{ji}\}
		$
		y representa el flujo que se puede enviar de $s$ a $j$.
		\begin{center}
			\begin{tikzpicture}[node_style/.style={draw,circle,minimum size=0.5cm,inner sep=1}]
				\node at (0,0.5) {$(k,\delta_i)$} ;
				\node at (3,0.5) {$(-i,\delta_j)$} ;
				\node[node_style] (i) at (0,0) {$i$} ;
				\node[node_style] (j) at (3,0) {$j$} ;
				\draw [line width=1.5pt] (i) edge[<-] node[above] {\textcolor{red}{$f_{ji}$}\textcolor{blue}{($c_{ji}$)}} (j) ;
			\end{tikzpicture}
		\end{center}
	\end{enumerate}
	Un vértice está explorado si está etiquetado o no se puede etiquetar desde ninguno de sus nodos adyacentes.
\end{frame}

\begin{frame}{Algoritmo de Ford-Fulkerson. Etiquetado de vértices}\small
	\begin{enumerate}\itemsep 1mm
		\item Asignar un flujo inicial (por ejemplo, $\vf=\vv$ y etiquetar $s$ con
		$(-,\delta_s=+\infty)$.
		\item ¿Todos los vértices no etiquetados han sido explorados? SI: paso 6. NO: paso 3.
		\item Etiquetar todos los vértices posibles con la regla anterior.
		\item ¿$t$ ha sido etiquetado? SI: $\Delta=\delta_t$ e ir al paso 5. NO: paso 2.
		\item Modificar el flujo actual:
		\begin{enumerate}\itemsep 0mm
			\item Hacer $k\leftarrow t$.
			\item Si la etiqueta del vértice $k$ es $(+i,\delta_k)$, $f_{ik}\leftarrow f_{ik}+\Delta$.
			\item Si la etiqueta del vértice $k$ es $(-i,\delta_k)$, $f_{ki}\leftarrow f_{ki}-\Delta$.
			\item Hacer $k\leftarrow i$. ¿$i=s$? SI: borrar todas las etiquetas (excepto la de $s$) e ir al paso 2. NO: ir al paso  5.2.
		\end{enumerate}
		\item El flujo actual es máximo. El corte de capacidad mínima esta definido por los
		vértices etiquetados.
	\end{enumerate}
	
\end{frame}

\begin{frame}
	
	\frametitle{Ejemplo}
	
	\begin{block}{Ejemplo}
		Encontrar el flujo m\'aximo del nodo $s$ al nodo $t$ e identificar el corte de capacidad m\'inima en la siguiente red de transporte usando el algoritmo de Ford-Fulkerson:
		\begin{center}
			\begin{tikzpicture}[node_style/.style={draw,circle,minimum size=0.5cm,inner sep=1}]
				\node[node_style] (s) at (0,1.5) {s} ;
				\node[node_style] (1) at (2,3) {1} ;
				\node[node_style] (2) at (2,0) {2} ;
				\node[node_style] (3) at (4,3) {3} ;
				\node[node_style] (4) at (4,0) {4} ;
				\node[node_style] (5) at (6,3) {5} ;
				\node[node_style] (6) at (6,0) {6} ;
				\node[node_style] (t) at (8,1.5) {t} ;
				
				\draw [thick] (s) edge[->] node[above] {(7)} (1) ;
				\draw [thick] (s) edge[->] node[below] {(2)} (2) ;
				\draw [thick] (1) edge[->] node[above] {(5)} (3) ;
				\draw [thick] (1) edge[->] node[left]  {(1)} (2) ;
				\draw [thick] (2) edge[->] node[below] {(4)} (4) ;
				\draw [thick] (3) edge[->] node[right] {(3)} (2) ;
				\draw [thick] (3) edge[->] node[right] {(2)} (4) ;
				\draw [thick] (3) edge[->] node[above] {(4)} (5) ;
				\draw [thick] (4) edge[->] node[below] {(5)} (6) ;
				\draw [thick] (5) edge[->] node[right] {(3)} (6) ;
				\draw [thick] (5) edge[->] node[above] {(3)} (t) ;
				\draw [thick] (6) edge[->] node[below] {(6)} (t) ;
			\end{tikzpicture}
		\end{center}
	\end{block}
	
\end{frame}


\begin{frame}
	
	\frametitle{Resoluci\'on}
	
	\only<1>{Iteraci\'on 1:}
	\only<2>{Iteraci\'on 2:}
	\only<3>{Iteraci\'on 3:}
	\only<4>{Iteraci\'on 4:}
	\only<5>{Iteraci\'on 5:}
	\only<6>{Iteraci\'on 6, s\'olo se pueden etiquetar $s$, $1$:}
	
	\begin{columns}
		\column{.5\textwidth}
		\begin{center}
			\resizebox{\textwidth}{!}{\begin{tikzpicture}[node_style/.style={draw,circle,minimum size=0.5cm,inner sep=1}]
					\node[node_style] (s) at (0,1.5) {s} ;
					\node[node_style] (1) at (2,3) {1} ;
					\node[node_style] (2) at (2,0) {2} ;
					\node[node_style] (3) at (4,3) {3} ;
					\node[node_style] (4) at (4,0) {4} ;
					\node[node_style] (5) at (6,3) {5} ;
					\node[node_style] (6) at (6,0) {6} ;
					\node[node_style] (t) at (8,1.5) {t} ;
					
					\onslide<1>{\draw[color=blue, very thick] (0,2.1) node {$(-,M)$};
						\draw[color=blue, very thick] (2,3.6) node {$(+s,7)$};
						\draw[color=blue, very thick] (2,-0.6) node {$(+s,2)$};
						\draw[color=blue, very thick] (4,3.6) node {$(+1,5)$};
						\draw[color=blue, very thick] (4,-0.6) node {$(+2,2)$};
						\draw[color=blue, very thick] (6,3.6) node {$(+3,4)$};
						\draw[color=blue, very thick] (6,-0.6) node {$(+4,2)$};
						\draw[color=blue, very thick] (8,2.1) node {$(+6,2)$};}
					
					\onslide<2>{\draw[color=blue, very thick] (0,2.1) node {$(-,M)$};
						\draw[color=blue, very thick] (2,3.6) node {$(+s,7)$};
						\draw[color=blue, very thick] (2,-0.6) node {$(+1,1)$};
						\draw[color=blue, very thick] (4,3.6) node {$(+1,5)$};
						\draw[color=blue, very thick] (4,-0.6) node {$(+3,2)$};
						\draw[color=blue, very thick] (6,3.6) node {$(+3,4)$};
						\draw[color=blue, very thick] (6,-0.6) node {$(+4,2)$};
						\draw[color=blue, very thick] (8,2.1) node {$(+5,3)$};}
					
					\onslide<3>{\draw[color=blue, very thick] (0,2.1) node {$(-,M)$};
						\draw[color=blue, very thick] (2,3.6) node {$(+s,4)$};
						\draw[color=blue, very thick] (2,-0.6) node {$(+1,1)$};
						\draw[color=blue, very thick] (4,3.6) node {$(+1,2)$};
						\draw[color=blue, very thick] (4,-0.6) node {$(+3,2)$};
						\draw[color=blue, very thick] (6,3.6) node {$(+3,1)$};
						\draw[color=blue, very thick] (6,-0.6) node {$(+5,1)$};
						\draw[color=blue, very thick] (8,2.1) node {$(+6,1)$};}
					
					\onslide<4>{\draw[color=blue, very thick] (0,2.1) node {$(-,M)$};
						\draw[color=blue, very thick] (2,3.6) node {$(+s,3)$};
						\draw[color=blue, very thick] (2,-0.6) node {$(+1,1)$};
						\draw[color=blue, very thick] (4,3.6) node {$(+1,1)$};
						\draw[color=blue, very thick] (4,-0.6) node {$(+3,1)$};
						\draw[color=blue, very thick] (6,3.6) node {$(-6,1)$};
						\draw[color=blue, very thick] (6,-0.6) node {$(+4,1)$};
						\draw[color=blue, very thick] (8,2.1) node {$(+6,1)$};}
					
					\onslide<5>{\draw[color=blue, very thick] (0,2.1) node {$(-,M)$};
						\draw[color=blue, very thick] (2,3.6) node {$(+s,2)$};
						\draw[color=blue, very thick] (2,-0.6) node {$(+1,1)$};
						\draw[color=blue, very thick] (4,3.6) node {$(-4,1)$};
						\draw[color=blue, very thick] (4,-0.6) node {$(+2,1)$};
						\draw[color=blue, very thick] (6,3.6) node {$(-6,1)$};
						\draw[color=blue, very thick] (6,-0.6) node {$(+4,1)$};
						\draw[color=blue, very thick] (8,2.1) node {$(+6,1)$};}
					
					\onslide<6>{\draw[color=blue, very thick] (0,2.1) node {$(-,M)$};
						\draw[color=blue, very thick] (2,3.6) node {$(+s,1)$};}
					
					\onslide<1>{\draw [thick] (s) edge[->] node[above] {(7),\textcolor{red}{0}} (1) ;
						\draw [thick] (s) edge[->] node[below] {(2),\textcolor{red}{0}} (2) ;
						\draw [thick] (1) edge[->] node[above]  {(5),\textcolor{red}{0}} (3) ;
						\draw [thick] (1) edge[->] node[left]  {(1),\textcolor{red}{0}} (2) ;
						\draw [thick] (2) edge[->] node[below]  {(4),\textcolor{red}{0}} (4) ;
						\draw [thick] (3) edge[->] node[right]  {(3),\textcolor{red}{0}} (2) ;
						\draw [thick] (3) edge[->] node[right] {(2),\textcolor{red}{0}} (4) ;
						\draw [thick] (3) edge[->] node[above]  {(4),\textcolor{red}{0}} (5) ;
						\draw [thick] (4) edge[->] node[below]  {(5),\textcolor{red}{0}} (6) ;
						\draw [thick] (5) edge[->] node[right]  {(3),\textcolor{red}{0}} (6) ;
						\draw [thick] (5) edge[->] node[above]  {(3),\textcolor{red}{0}} (t) ;
						\draw [thick] (6) edge[->] node[below]  {(6),\textcolor{red}{0}} (t) ;}
					
					\onslide<2>{\draw [thick] (s) edge[->] node[above] {(7),\textcolor{red}{0}} (1) ;
						\draw [thick] (s) edge[->] node[below] {(2),\textcolor{red}{2}} (2) ;
						\draw [thick] (1) edge[->] node[above]  {(5),\textcolor{red}{0}} (3) ;
						\draw [thick] (1) edge[->] node[left]  {(1),\textcolor{red}{0}} (2) ;
						\draw [thick] (2) edge[->] node[below]  {(4),\textcolor{red}{2}} (4) ;
						\draw [thick] (3) edge[->] node[right]  {(3),\textcolor{red}{0}} (2) ;
						\draw [thick] (3) edge[->] node[right] {(2),\textcolor{red}{0}} (4) ;
						\draw [thick] (3) edge[->] node[above]  {(4),\textcolor{red}{0}} (5) ;
						\draw [thick] (4) edge[->] node[below]  {(5),\textcolor{red}{2}} (6) ;
						\draw [thick] (5) edge[->] node[right]  {(3),\textcolor{red}{0}} (6) ;
						\draw [thick] (5) edge[->] node[above]  {(3),\textcolor{red}{0}} (t) ;
						\draw [thick] (6) edge[->] node[below]  {(6),\textcolor{red}{2}} (t) ;}
					
					\onslide<3>{\draw [thick] (s) edge[->] node[above] {(7),\textcolor{red}{3}} (1) ;
						\draw [thick] (s) edge[->] node[below] {(2),\textcolor{red}{2}} (2) ;
						\draw [thick] (1) edge[->] node[above]  {(5),\textcolor{red}{3}} (3) ;
						\draw [thick] (1) edge[->] node[left]  {(1),\textcolor{red}{0}} (2) ;
						\draw [thick] (2) edge[->] node[below]  {(4),\textcolor{red}{2}} (4) ;
						\draw [thick] (3) edge[->] node[right]  {(3),\textcolor{red}{0}} (2) ;
						\draw [thick] (3) edge[->] node[right] {(2),\textcolor{red}{0}} (4) ;
						\draw [thick] (3) edge[->] node[above]  {(4),\textcolor{red}{3}} (5) ;
						\draw [thick] (4) edge[->] node[below]  {(5),\textcolor{red}{2}} (6) ;
						\draw [thick] (5) edge[->] node[right]  {(3),\textcolor{red}{0}} (6) ;
						\draw [thick] (5) edge[->] node[above]  {(3),\textcolor{red}{3}} (t) ;
						\draw [thick] (6) edge[->] node[below]  {(6),\textcolor{red}{2}} (t) ;}
					
					\onslide<4>{\draw [thick] (s) edge[->] node[above] {(7),\textcolor{red}{4}} (1) ;
						\draw [thick] (s) edge[->] node[below] {(2),\textcolor{red}{2}} (2) ;
						\draw [thick] (1) edge[->] node[above]  {(5),\textcolor{red}{4}} (3) ;
						\draw [thick] (1) edge[->] node[left]  {(1),\textcolor{red}{0}} (2) ;
						\draw [thick] (2) edge[->] node[below]  {(4),\textcolor{red}{2}} (4) ;
						\draw [thick] (3) edge[->] node[right]  {(3),\textcolor{red}{0}} (2) ;
						\draw [thick] (3) edge[->] node[right] {(2),\textcolor{red}{0}} (4) ;
						\draw [thick] (3) edge[->] node[above]  {(4),\textcolor{red}{4}} (5) ;
						\draw [thick] (4) edge[->] node[below]  {(5),\textcolor{red}{2}} (6) ;
						\draw [thick] (5) edge[->] node[right]  {(3),\textcolor{red}{1}} (6) ;
						\draw [thick] (5) edge[->] node[above]  {(3),\textcolor{red}{3}} (t) ;
						\draw [thick] (6) edge[->] node[below]  {(6),\textcolor{red}{3}} (t) ;}
					
					\onslide<5>{\draw [thick] (s) edge[->] node[above] {(7),\textcolor{red}{5}} (1) ;
						\draw [thick] (s) edge[->] node[below] {(2),\textcolor{red}{2}} (2) ;
						\draw [thick] (1) edge[->] node[above]  {(5),\textcolor{red}{5}} (3) ;
						\draw [thick] (1) edge[->] node[left]  {(1),\textcolor{red}{0}} (2) ;
						\draw [thick] (2) edge[->] node[below]  {(4),\textcolor{red}{2}} (4) ;
						\draw [thick] (3) edge[->] node[right]  {(3),\textcolor{red}{0}} (2) ;
						\draw [thick] (3) edge[->] node[right] {(2),\textcolor{red}{1}} (4) ;
						\draw [thick] (3) edge[->] node[above]  {(4),\textcolor{red}{4}} (5) ;
						\draw [thick] (4) edge[->] node[below]  {(5),\textcolor{red}{3}} (6) ;
						\draw [thick] (5) edge[->] node[right]  {(3),\textcolor{red}{1}} (6) ;
						\draw [thick] (5) edge[->] node[above]  {(3),\textcolor{red}{3}} (t) ;
						\draw [thick] (6) edge[->] node[below]  {(6),\textcolor{red}{4}} (t) ;}
					
					\onslide<6>{\draw [thick] (s) edge[->] node[above] {(7),\textcolor{red}{6}} (1) ;
						\draw [thick] (s) edge[->] node[below] {(2),\textcolor{red}{2}} (2) ;
						\draw [thick] (1) edge[->] node[above]  {(5),\textcolor{red}{5}} (3) ;
						\draw [thick] (1) edge[->] node[left]  {(1),\textcolor{red}{1}} (2) ;
						\draw [thick] (2) edge[->] node[below]  {(4),\textcolor{red}{3}} (4) ;
						\draw [thick] (3) edge[->] node[right]  {(3),\textcolor{red}{0}} (2) ;
						\draw [thick] (3) edge[->] node[right] {(2),\textcolor{red}{1}} (4) ;
						\draw [thick] (3) edge[->] node[above]  {(4),\textcolor{red}{4}} (5) ;
						\draw [thick] (4) edge[->] node[below]  {(5),\textcolor{red}{4}} (6) ;
						\draw [thick] (5) edge[->] node[right]  {(3),\textcolor{red}{1}} (6) ;
						\draw [thick] (5) edge[->] node[above]  {(3),\textcolor{red}{3}} (t) ;
						\draw [thick] (6) edge[->] node[below]  {(6),\textcolor{red}{5}} (t) ;}
					
			\end{tikzpicture}}
			
		\end{center}
		
		\column{.5\textwidth}
		\begin{center}
			\resizebox{\textwidth}{!}{\begin{tikzpicture}[node_style/.style={draw,circle,minimum size=0.5cm,inner sep=1}]
					\onslide<1-5>{\node[node_style] (s) at (0,1.5) {s} ;
						\node[node_style] (1) at (2,3) {1} ;
						\node[node_style] (2) at (2,0) {2} ;
						\node[node_style] (3) at (4,3) {3} ;
						\node[node_style] (4) at (4,0) {4} ;
						\node[node_style] (5) at (6,3) {5} ;
						\node[node_style] (6) at (6,0) {6} ;
						\node[node_style] (t) at (8,1.5) {t} ;}
					
					\onslide<6>{\node[node_style,color=orange] (s) at (0,1.5) {s} ;
						\node[node_style,color=orange] (1) at (2,3) {1} ;
						\node[node_style,color=limegreen] (2) at (2,0) {2} ;
						\node[node_style,color=limegreen] (3) at (4,3) {3} ;
						\node[node_style,color=limegreen] (4) at (4,0) {4} ;
						\node[node_style,color=limegreen] (5) at (6,3) {5} ;
						\node[node_style,color=limegreen] (6) at (6,0) {6} ;
						\node[node_style,color=limegreen] (t) at (8,1.5) {t} ;}
					
					\onslide<1>{\draw [thick](s) edge[->] node[above] {(7),\textcolor{red}{0}} (1) ;
						\draw [thick](s) edge[->] node[below] {(2),\textcolor{red}{2}} (2) ;
						\draw [thick](1) edge[->] node[above]  {(5),\textcolor{red}{0}} (3) ;
						\draw [thick](1) edge[->] node[left]  {(1),\textcolor{red}{0}} (2) ;
						\draw [thick](2) edge[->] node[below]  {(4),\textcolor{red}{2}} (4) ;
						\draw [thick](3) edge[->] node[right]  {(3),\textcolor{red}{0}} (2) ;
						\draw [thick](3) edge[->] node[right] {(2),\textcolor{red}{0}} (4) ;
						\draw [thick](3) edge[->] node[above]  {(4),\textcolor{red}{0}} (5) ;
						\draw [thick](4) edge[->] node[below]  {(5),\textcolor{red}{2}} (6) ;
						\draw [thick](5) edge[->] node[right]  {(3),\textcolor{red}{0}} (6) ;
						\draw [thick](5) edge[->] node[above]  {(3),\textcolor{red}{0}} (t) ;
						\draw [thick](6) edge[->] node[below]  {(6),\textcolor{red}{2}} (t) ;}
					
					\onslide<2>{\draw [thick](s) edge[->] node[above] {(7),\textcolor{red}{3}} (1) ;
						\draw [thick](s) edge[->] node[below] {(2),\textcolor{red}{2}} (2) ;
						\draw [thick](1) edge[->] node[above]  {(5),\textcolor{red}{3}} (3) ;
						\draw [thick](1) edge[->] node[left]  {(1),\textcolor{red}{0}} (2) ;
						\draw [thick](2) edge[->] node[below]  {(4),\textcolor{red}{2}} (4) ;
						\draw [thick](3) edge[->] node[right]  {(3),\textcolor{red}{0}} (2) ;
						\draw [thick](3) edge[->] node[right] {(2),\textcolor{red}{0}} (4) ;
						\draw [thick](3) edge[->] node[above]  {(4),\textcolor{red}{3}} (5) ;
						\draw [thick](4) edge[->] node[below]  {(5),\textcolor{red}{2}} (6) ;
						\draw [thick](5) edge[->] node[right]  {(3),\textcolor{red}{0}} (6) ;
						\draw [thick](5) edge[->] node[above]  {(3),\textcolor{red}{3}} (t) ;
						\draw [thick](6) edge[->] node[below]  {(6),\textcolor{red}{2}} (t) ;}
					
					\onslide<3>{\draw [thick](s) edge[->] node[above] {(7),\textcolor{red}{4}} (1) ;
						\draw [thick](s) edge[->] node[below] {(2),\textcolor{red}{2}} (2) ;
						\draw [thick](1) edge[->] node[above]  {(5),\textcolor{red}{4}} (3) ;
						\draw [thick](1) edge[->] node[left]  {(1),\textcolor{red}{0}} (2) ;
						\draw [thick](2) edge[->] node[below]  {(4),\textcolor{red}{2}} (4) ;
						\draw [thick](3) edge[->] node[right]  {(3),\textcolor{red}{0}} (2) ;
						\draw [thick](3) edge[->] node[right] {(2),\textcolor{red}{0}} (4) ;
						\draw [thick](3) edge[->] node[above]  {(4),\textcolor{red}{4}} (5) ;
						\draw [thick](4) edge[->] node[below]  {(5),\textcolor{red}{2}} (6) ;
						\draw [thick](5) edge[->] node[right]  {(3),\textcolor{red}{1}} (6) ;
						\draw [thick](5) edge[->] node[above]  {(3),\textcolor{red}{3}} (t) ;
						\draw [thick](6) edge[->] node[below]  {(6),\textcolor{red}{3}} (t) ;}
					
					\onslide<4>{\draw [thick](s) edge[->] node[above] {(7),\textcolor{red}{5}} (1) ;
						\draw [thick](s) edge[->] node[below] {(2),\textcolor{red}{2}} (2) ;
						\draw [thick](1) edge[->] node[above]  {(5),\textcolor{red}{5}} (3) ;
						\draw [thick](1) edge[->] node[left]  {(1),\textcolor{red}{0}} (2) ;
						\draw [thick](2) edge[->] node[below]  {(4),\textcolor{red}{2}} (4) ;
						\draw [thick](3) edge[->] node[right]  {(3),\textcolor{red}{0}} (2) ;
						\draw [thick](3) edge[->] node[right] {(2),\textcolor{red}{1}} (4) ;
						\draw [thick](3) edge[->] node[above]  {(4),\textcolor{red}{4}} (5) ;
						\draw [thick](4) edge[->] node[below]  {(5),\textcolor{red}{3}} (6) ;
						\draw [thick](5) edge[->] node[right]  {(3),\textcolor{red}{1}} (6) ;
						\draw [thick](5) edge[->] node[above]  {(3),\textcolor{red}{3}} (t) ;
						\draw [thick](6) edge[->] node[below]  {(6),\textcolor{red}{4}} (t) ;}
					
					\onslide<5>{\draw [thick](s) edge[->] node[above] {(7),\textcolor{red}{6}} (1) ;
						\draw [thick](s) edge[->] node[below] {(2),\textcolor{red}{2}} (2) ;
						\draw [thick](1) edge[->] node[above]  {(5),\textcolor{red}{5}} (3) ;
						\draw [thick](1) edge[->] node[left]  {(1),\textcolor{red}{1}} (2) ;
						\draw [thick](2) edge[->] node[below]  {(4),\textcolor{red}{3}} (4) ;
						\draw [thick](3) edge[->] node[right]  {(3),\textcolor{red}{0}} (2) ;
						\draw [thick](3) edge[->] node[right] {(2),\textcolor{red}{1}} (4) ;
						\draw [thick](3) edge[->] node[above]  {(4),\textcolor{red}{4}} (5) ;
						\draw [thick](4) edge[->] node[below]  {(5),\textcolor{red}{4}} (6) ;
						\draw [thick](5) edge[->] node[right]  {(3),\textcolor{red}{1}} (6) ;
						\draw [thick](5) edge[->] node[above]  {(3),\textcolor{red}{3}} (t) ;
						\draw [thick](6) edge[->] node[below]  {(6),\textcolor{red}{5}} (t) ;}
					
					\onslide<6>{\draw [thick](s) edge[->] node[above] {(7),\textcolor{red}{6}} (1) ;
						\draw [thick](s) edge[->,color=blue,line width=1.3pt] node[below] {(2),\textcolor{red}{2}} (2) ;
						\draw [thick](1) edge[->,color=blue,line width=1.3pt] node[above]  {(5),\textcolor{red}{5}} (3) ;
						\draw [thick](1) edge[->,color=blue,line width=1.3pt] node[left]  {(1),\textcolor{red}{1}} (2) ;
						\draw [thick](2) edge[->] node[below]  {(4),\textcolor{red}{3}} (4) ;
						\draw [thick](3) edge[->] node[right]  {(3),\textcolor{red}{0}} (2) ;
						\draw [thick](3) edge[->] node[right] {(2),\textcolor{red}{1}} (4) ;
						\draw [thick](3) edge[->] node[above]  {(4),\textcolor{red}{4}} (5) ;
						\draw [thick](4) edge[->] node[below]  {(5),\textcolor{red}{4}} (6) ;
						\draw [thick](5) edge[->] node[right]  {(3),\textcolor{red}{1}} (6) ;
						\draw [thick](5) edge[->] node[above]  {(3),\textcolor{red}{3}} (t) ;
						\draw [thick](6) edge[->] node[below]  {(6),\textcolor{red}{5}} (t) ;}
					
			\end{tikzpicture}}
		\end{center}
	\end{columns}
	
	\visible<6>{El corte divide el grafo con los v\'ertices $s$ y $1$ por un lado, es decir, los arcos $(s,2)$, $(1,3)$ y $(1,2)$}
\end{frame}



\begin{frame}{Algoritmo de Ford-Fulkerson}
	Aplicar el algoritmo a la siguiente red de transporte:
	\begin{center}\vspace*{-5mm}
		\begin{tikzpicture}[node_style/.style={draw,circle,minimum size=0.5cm,inner sep=1}]
			\node[node_style] (s) at (0,1.5) {s} ;
			\node[node_style] (b) at (3,3) {b} ;
			\node[node_style] (c) at (3,0) {c} ;
			\node[node_style] (d) at (6,3) {d} ;
			\node[node_style] (e) at (6,0) {e} ;
			\node[node_style] (t) at (9,1.5) {t} ;
			\draw  [line width=1.5pt] (s) edge[->] node[above] {(5)} (b) ;
			\draw  [line width=1.5pt] (s) edge[->] node[above] {(3)} (c) ;
			\draw  [line width=1.5pt] (b) edge[->,bend right=8] node[below] {(4)} (d) ;
			\draw  [line width=1.5pt] (b) edge[->] node[left] {(3)} (c) ;
			\draw  [line width=1.5pt] (c) edge[->,bend right=8] node[below] {(3)} (e) ;
			\draw  [line width=1.5pt] (c) edge[->,bend right=8] node[below] {(4)} (d) ;
			\draw  [line width=1.5pt] (d) edge[->,bend right=8] node[above] {(2)} (b) ;
			\draw  [line width=1.5pt] (d) edge[->,bend right=8] node[above] {(1)} (c) ;
			\draw  [line width=1.5pt] (d) edge[->,bend right=8] node[left] {(3)} (e) ;
			\draw  [line width=1.5pt] (d) edge[->] node[above] {(5)} (t) ;
			\draw  [line width=1.5pt] (e) edge[->,bend right=8] node[above] {(1)} (c) ;
			\draw  [line width=1.5pt] (e) edge[->,bend right=8] node[right] {(3)} (d) ;
			\draw  [line width=1.5pt] (e) edge[->,bend right=8] node[below] {(5)} (t) ;
			\draw  [line width=1.5pt] (t) edge[->,bend right=8] node[above] {(1)} (e) ;
			%	\draw  [line width=1.5pt] (t) edge[->,bend left=60] node[below] {8} (s) ;
		\end{tikzpicture}
	\end{center}	
	Solución óptima: $f_0=8$.
\end{frame}


\begin{frame}{Algoritmo de Ford-Fulkerson}
	\begin{itemize}\itemsep 2mm
		\item Si las capacidades son números enteros, la solución es entera.
		\item En algún caso, con capacidades irracionales no se ha logrado convergencia finita del algoritmo. 
		\item La complejidad depende del paso 3 (que tiene complejidad $m$) y de $v$, número de incrementos.
		\item Como en cada iteración se incrementa al menos una unidad, $v$ suele estar acotado por el máximo de las capacidades de los arcos. 
	\end{itemize}
	\begin{block}{Teorema de Edmods y Karp (1972) (publicado previamente por Dinitz (1970))}
		Si cada incremeto de flujo se realiza por la cadena con menor número de arcos en el grafo incremental, el flujo máximo se obtiene con no mas de $\frac{1}{2}mn$ incrementos de flujo.
	\end{block}
\end{frame}

\subsection{Modelo de Optimización Lineal}

\begin{frame}{Formulaci\'on de optimización lineal}
	
	El problema de flujo m\'aximo entre los v\'ertices $s$ y $t$ de una red de transporte puede modelarse del siguiente modo:
	\begin{itemize}\itemsep 3mm
		\item \alert{Datos:}   $i = 1,\ldots,n,s,t$, vértices de la red de transporte y $c_{ij}$, capacidad del arco $(i,j)$.
		\item \alert{Variables:} $f_{ij}$, flujo enviado del vértice $i$ al vértice $j$.
		\item \alert{Modelo:}
	\begin{align*}
	\max\;\;     & f_0 \\
	\textrm{s.a.:\;\;} &   \sum_{j:(j,i)\in V} f_{ji} - \sum_{j:(i,j) \in V} f_{ij} = \begin{cases}-f_0 & \textrm{si }i=s\\ 0 & \textrm{si }i \neq s,t\\ f_0 & \textrm{si }i=t\end{cases} \\
	&   0 \leqslant f_{ij} \leqslant c_{ij} \quad \forall (i,j) \in V
	\end{align*}
	\end{itemize}
\end{frame}

\begin{frame}{Formulaci\'on de programaci\'on lineal}{Ejemplo}
	\begin{columns}
		\column{0.5\textwidth}
		\begin{center}
			\begin{tikzpicture}[node_style/.style={draw,circle,minimum size=0.5cm,inner sep=1}]
				\node[node_style] (1) at (2.5,2.5) {1} ;
				\node[node_style] (t) at (5,0) {t} ;
				\only<1>{
					\node[node_style] (s) at (0,0) {s} ;
					\draw [thick] (s) edge[->] node[below] {$x_{st}$ $c_{st}=2$} (t) ;
					\draw [thick] (s) edge[->] node[left] {$c_{s1}=3$} node[right] {$x_{s1}$} (1) ;
					\draw [thick] (1) edge[->] node[right] {$c_{1t}=4$} node[left] {$x_{1t}$} (t) ;
				}
				
				
				\only<2>{
					\node[node_style] (s) at (0,0) {s} ;
					\draw [thick,blue] (s) edge[->] node[below] {$x_{st}=2$ $c_{st}=2$} (t) ;
					\draw [thick,blue] (s) edge[->] node[left] {$c_{s1}=3$} node[right] {$x_{s1}=3$} (1) ;
					\draw [thick] (1) edge[->] node[right] {$c_{1t}=4$} node[below left] {$x_{1t}=3$} (t) ;
				}
				
				
			\end{tikzpicture}
		\end{center}
		\column{0.5\textwidth}
		\begin{align*}
			max\, & f_0 
			\\
			\text{s.a. }\, & 0-x_{s1}-x_{st} = - f_0 & (i=s)
			\\
			& x_{s1}-x_{1t}=0 & (i=1)
			\\
			& x_{st}+x_{1t} = f_0
			\\
			&x_{s1} \leq 3
			\\
			&x_{st} \leq 2
			\\
			&x_{1t} \leq 4
			\\
			&x_{s1},x_{st},x_{1t} \geq 0
		\end{align*}
	\end{columns}
	
	\bigskip
	
	\visible<2>{Solución óptima: $f_0=5$, $x_{s1}=x_{1t}=3$, $x_{st}=2$. \quad Corte: $\{(s,1),(s,t)\}$ }
\end{frame}


\begin{frame}{Formulaci\'on de programaci\'on lineal}{Ejemplo}
	\begin{columns}
		\column{0.35\textwidth}
		\begin{align*}
			\max\, & f_0 
			\\
			\text{sujeto a}\quad & 0-x_{s1}-x_{st} = - f_0 
			\\
			& x_{s1}-x_{1t}=0 
			\\
			& x_{st}+x_{1t} = f_0
			\\
			&x_{s1} \leq 3
			\\
			&x_{st} \leq 2
			\\
			&x_{1t} \leq 4
			\\
			&x_{s1},x_{st},x_{1t} \geq 0
		\end{align*}
		\column{0.55\textwidth}
		\alert{Variables del dual:}
		\begin{itemize}
			\item $u_i$, $i\in V$, asociadas a la ley de conservación de flujo. 
			\item $w_{ij}$, $(i,j)\in U$ asociadas a la capacidad.
		\end{itemize}
		\alert{Formulación del dual}
		\begin{align*}
			\min\, & 3w_{s1}+2w_{st}+ 4 w_{1t} 
			\\
			\text{sujeto a}\quad, & u_s-u_t = 1
			\\
			& u_1-u_s+w_{s1} \geq 0
			\\
			& u_t-u_s+w_{st} \geq 0
			\\
			& u_t-u_1+w_{1t} \geq 0
			\\
			& w_{s1},w_{st},w_{1t} \geq 0
		\end{align*}
	\end{columns}
\end{frame}

\begin{frame}{Flujo máximo y corte de capacidad mínima. Relación}
	Sea el modelo matemático para el problema de flujo
	máximo:
	\begin{align*}
		\max\;\;     & f_0 \\
		\text{sujeto a}\quad  &   \sum_{j:(j,i)\in U} f_{ji} - \sum_{j:(i,j) \in U} f_{ij} = \begin{cases}-f_0 & \textrm{si }i=s\\ 0 & \textrm{si }i \neq s,t\\ f_0 & \textrm{si }i=t\end{cases} \\
		&   0 \leqslant f_{ij} \leqslant \overline{f}_{ij} \quad \forall (i,j) \in U
	\end{align*}
	
	\noindent El dual de este modelo es:
	\begin{align*}
		\min &\sum_{(i,j)\in U} \overline{f}_{ij}w_{ij}
		\\
		\text{sujeto a }\quad & u_j-u_i+w_{ij} \geq 0, &&\forall (i,j)\in U,
		\\
		&  v_s-v_t = 1,                   
		\\
		&  w_{ij}\geq 0,   && \forall (i,j)\in U,      
		\\
		& v_i\text{ no restringida,} && \forall i\in V.
	\end{align*}
\end{frame}

\begin{frame}{Flujo máximo y corte de capacidad mínima. Relación}
	Ecuaciones de conservación de flujo del problema primal:
	$$
	\sum_{j:(j,i)\in U} f_{ji} - \sum_{j:(i,j) \in U} f_{ij} =
	\begin{cases}
		-f_0 & \textrm{si }i=s\\
		0 & \textrm{si }i \neq s,t\\ 
		f_0 & \textrm{si }i=t
	\end{cases} 
	$$
	Una es redundante y se puede eliminar. Seleccionamos la asociada al nodo $t$. Su correspondiente variable del dual se anula ($u_t=0$) y por tanto, $u_s=1$. En el ejemplo resulta:
	\begin{columns}
		\column{0.4\textwidth}
		\begin{align*}
			\min\, & 3w_{s1}+2w_{st}+ 4 w_{1t} 
			\\
			\text{s.a. }\, & 
			\\
			& u_1+w_{s1} \geq 1
			\\
			& w_{st} \geq 1
			\\
			&-u_1+w_{1t} \geq 0
			\\
			& w_{s1},w_{st},w_{1t} \geq 0
		\end{align*} 
		\column{0.5\textwidth}
		Solución óptima:
		\begin{align*}
			& w_{s1}=1 &\quad u_1=0 \\
			& w_{st}=1 &\quad u_s=1 \\
			& w_{1t}=0 & \quad u_t=0 
		\end{align*}
		Definen precisamente el corte de capacidad mínima.
	\end{columns}
\end{frame}

\section[Flujo compatible]{Problema de flujo con coste m\'inimo}

\begin{frame}{Flujo con coste mínimo}\small
Sea un problema de asignación planteado como un problema de flujo
  \begin{center}
	\begin{tikzpicture}[node_style/.style={draw,circle,minimum size=0.5cm,inner sep=1}]
	\node[node_style] (s) at (0,2) {s} ;
	\node (x1) at (3,4.5) {Máquinas} ;
	\node (x2) at (6,4.5) {Tareas} ;
	\node (x3) at (4.5,4.25) {$c_a=1$} ;
	\node (o1) at (3,4) {$m_1$} ;
	\node (o2) at (3,3) {$m_2$} ;
	\node (o3) at (3,2) {$\vdots$} ;
	\node (o4) at (3,1) {} ;
	\node (o5) at (3,0) {$m_n$} ;
	\node (t1) at (6,4) {$t_1$} ;
	\node (t2) at (6,3) {$t_2$} ;
	\node (t3) at (6,2) {$\vdots$} ;
	\node (t4) at (6,1) {} ;
	\node (t5) at (6,0) {$t_n$} ;
	\node[node_style] (t) at (8,2) {t} ;
	\draw [thick] (s) edge[->]  (o1) ;
	\draw [thick] (s) edge[->]  (o2) ;
	\draw [thick] (s) edge[->]  (o4) ;
	\draw [thick] (s) edge[->]  (o5) ;
	\draw [thick] (t1) edge[->]  (t) ;
	\draw [thick] (t2) edge[->]   (t) ;
	\draw [thick] (t4) edge[->]  (t) ;
	\draw [thick] (t5) edge[->]  (t) ;
	\draw [thick] (t4) edge[->]  (t) ;
	\draw [thick] (o1) edge[->]  (t1) ;
	\draw [thick] (o1) edge[->]  (t2) ;
	\draw [thick] (o1) edge[->]  (t4) ;
%	\draw [thick] (o1) edge[->]  (t5) ;
	\draw [thick] (o2) edge[->]  (t1) ;
	\draw [thick] (o2) edge[->]  (t2) ;
	\draw [thick] (o2) edge[->]  (t4) ;
	\draw [thick] (o2) edge[->]  (t5) ;
%	\draw [thick] (o5) edge[->]  (t1) ;
	\draw [thick] (o5) edge[->]  (t2) ;
	\draw [thick] (o5) edge[->]  (t4) ;
	\draw [thick] (o5) edge[->]  (t5) ;
	\end{tikzpicture}
	
	Cada máquina se une mediante u arco con las tareas que puede realizar.
\end{center}

La solución del problema de flujo máximo determina el número de tareas que se pueden realizar. 

El problema se complica si cada asignación tiene un coste diferente.
\end{frame}

\begin{frame}{Flujo compatible a coste mínimo}

Sea una red de transporte $G=(V,U)$, se incorpora, además de la capacidad de cada arco, un coste unitario $p_{ij}$, $(i,j)\in U$, por el flujo que transita a través de él. Fijados los nodos fuente $s$ y sumidero $t$ y un flujo $\nu$ de $s$ a $t$, el problema consiste en encontrar un flujo compatible a coste mínimo:
$$
0\leq f_u\leq c_u\quad \forall u\in U,\quad\text{tal que}\quad f_0=\nu
$$
tal que:
$$
\min  \sum_{i=1}^n\sum_{j=1}^n p_{ij}x_{ij},
$$
\end{frame}


\begin{frame}{Flujo compatible a coste mínimo}
\visible<2->{Para $\nu=4$ hay dos soluciones:}
  \begin{center}
	\begin{tikzpicture}[node_style/.style={draw,circle,minimum size=0.5cm,inner sep=1},noded/.style={fill=lightgray,circle,inner sep=0.5}]

			
	\node[node_style] (s) at (0,1.5) {s} ;
	\node[node_style] (a) at (3,3) {a} ;
	\node[node_style] (b) at (3,0) {b} ;
	\node[node_style] (c) at (6,2.5) {c} ;
	\node[node_style] (t) at (8,0) {t} ;
	\only<1>{
		\draw [thick] (s) edge[->] node[noded] {\tiny\color{black} 1} node[above]{(3)[3]} (a) ;
		\draw [thick] (s) edge[->] node[noded] {\tiny\color{black} 2} node[below]{(4)[1]} (b);
		\draw [thick] (a) edge[->] node[noded] {\tiny\color{black} 3} node[above]{(3)[2]} (c);
		\draw [thick] (b) edge[->]node[noded] {\tiny\color{black} 4}  node[left]{(1)[5]} (c);
		\draw [thick] (b) edge[->] node[noded] {\tiny\color{black} 5} node[below]{(4)[2]} (t);
		\draw [thick] (c) edge[->] node[noded] {\tiny\color{black} 6} node[right]{(3)[1]} (t);
	}
	\only<2->{
		\draw [thick] (s) edge[->] node[noded] {\tiny\color{black} 1} node[above]{(3)[3]} node[below]{ {\color{red} 1} \, {\color{blue} 2} } (a) ;
		\draw [thick] (s) edge[->] node[noded] {\tiny\color{black} 2} node[below]{(4)[1]} node[above]{ {\color{red} 3}\, {\color{blue} 2}} (b) ;
		\draw [thick] (a) edge[->] node[noded] {\tiny\color{black} 3}  node[above]{(3)[2]} node[below]{ {\color{red} 1}\, {\color{blue} 2}} (c) ;
		\draw [thick] (b) edge[->] node[noded] {\tiny\color{black} 4}  node[left]{(1)[5]} node[right]{ {\color{red} 1}\, {\color{blue} 0}} (c) ;
		\draw [thick] (b) edge[->] node[noded] {\tiny\color{black} 5}  node[below]{(4)[2]} node[above]{{\color{red} 2}\, {\color{blue} 2}} (t) ;
		\draw [thick] (c) edge[->] node[noded] {\tiny\color{black} 6} node[right]{(3)[1]} node[left]{{\color{red} 2}\, {\color{blue} 2}} (t) ;
 	}
	\only<2>{\node (d1) at (9,3) {\color{red} A: $c_A=19$}};
	\only<2>{\node (d2) at (9,2.5) {\color{blue} B: $c_B=18$}};
	\end{tikzpicture}
\end{center}
\visible<3->{
	\begin{align*}
	\vf^A & = (1,3,1,1,2,2)
	\\
	\vf^B & = (2,2,2,0,2,2)
	\\
	\mu^1=\vf^A-\vf^B & = (-1,1,-1,1,0,0) \qquad c_{A-B}=1
	\end{align*}
}
\end{frame}
\begin{frame}{Flujo compatible a coste mínimo}
La diferencia entre las dos soluciones es un ciclo.

Por tanto, es posible pasar de $\vf^A$ a $\vf^B$ \alert{\em sumando} un ciclo. 

\begin{block}{Definición}
El coste de una cadena es la suma del coste de los arcos que la componen (considerando el signo de la orientación del arco dentro de la cadena)
\end{block}
\begin{block}{Definición}
Una cadena incremental	es una cadena que admite un incremento de flujo. 
\end{block}
A partir de $\vf^A$, $\mu^1$ NO es cadena incremental, pero $-\mu^1$ SÍ.
\end{frame}

\begin{frame}{Flujo compatible a coste mínimo}
\begin{block}{Teorema 1}
Un flujo con valor $f_0=\nu$ es de coste mínimo si y solo si  no admite ciclos incrementales que no incluyan el arco $(s,t)$ de coste negativo 
\end{block}
\alert{Demostración:} se basa en que un flujo es una combinación lineal de ciclos.
\end{frame}
\begin{frame}{Flujo compatible a coste mínimo}
En este ejemplo hay tres ciclos (con dos orientaciones cada uno).
\begin{center}
	\begin{tikzpicture}[node_style/.style={draw,circle,minimum size=0.5cm,inner sep=1},noded/.style={fill=lightgray,circle,inner sep=0.5}]
	\node[node_style] (s) at (0,1.5) {s} ;
	\node[node_style] (a) at (3,3) {a} ;
	\node[node_style] (b) at (3,0) {b} ;
	\node[node_style] (c) at (6,2.5) {c} ;
	\node[node_style] (t) at (8,0) {t} ;
		\draw [thick] (s) edge[->] node[noded] {\tiny\color{black} 1} node[above]{(3)[3]} node[below]{ {\color{red} 1} } (a) ;
		\draw [thick] (s) edge[->] node[noded] {\tiny\color{black} 2} node[below]{(4)[1]} node[above]{ {\color{red} 3}} (b) ;
		\draw [thick] (a) edge[->] node[noded] {\tiny\color{black} 3}  node[above]{(3)[2]} node[below]{ {\color{red} 1}} (c) ;
		\draw [thick] (b) edge[->] node[noded] {\tiny\color{black} 4} node[left]{(1)[5]} node[right]{ {\color{red} 1} } (c) ;
		\draw [thick] (b) edge[->] node[noded] {\tiny\color{black} 5}  node[below]{(4)[2]} node[above]{{\color{red} 2}} (t) ;
		\draw [thick] (c) edge[->] node[noded] {\tiny\color{black} 6} node[right]{(3)[1]} node[left]{{\color{red} 2}} (t) ;
	\end{tikzpicture}
\end{center}
¿Hay algún ciclo incremental? ¿Tiene coste negativo?
\end{frame}

\begin{frame}{Flujo compatible a coste mínimo}
\begin{block}{Teorema 2}
Dado un flujo $\vf_0$ con valor $\nu$ y de coste mínimo, si se incrementa en $\delta$ unidades el flujo $\vf_0$ en los arcos de la cadena de $s$ a $t$ con coste mínimo, se obtiene el flujo de coste mínimo para el valor del flujo $\nu+\delta$.
\end{block}
Es decir, si tenemos la solución óptima para in determinado flujo $\vf_0$, podemos ir incrementando el flujo de $s$ a $t$, obteniendo en cada caso la solución óptima para el nuevo flujo.
\end{frame}

\begin{frame}{Flujo compatible a coste mínimo}
Se puede iniciar el proceso con el flujo nulo ($\vf_0=0$), aunque si la red tiene un circuito incremental de coste negativo, la solución no es óptima.

\medskip

Nota: si los costes son no negativos, no existe tal circuito.

\medskip

Aplicando el teorema 1 se puede encontrar la solución optima inicial.

\medskip

Aplicando el teorema 2 se puede ir incrementando el flujo de $s$ a $t$ hasta encontrar el valor de flujo $\nu$  prefijado en el problema. 
\end{frame}
\begin{frame}{Flujo compatible a coste mínimo}
En este ejemplo el flujo nulo es óptimo para $\nu_0=0$. Se busca la solución a coste mínimo para $\nu=4$.
\begin{center}
	\begin{tikzpicture}[node_style/.style={draw,circle,minimum size=0.5cm,inner sep=1},noded/.style={fill=lightgray,circle,inner sep=0.5}]
	\node[node_style] (s) at (0,1.5) {s} ;
	\node[node_style] (a) at (3,3) {a} ;
	\node[node_style] (b) at (3,0) {b} ;
	\node[node_style] (c) at (6,2.5) {c} ;
	\node[node_style] (t) at (8,0) {t} ;
	\draw [thick] (s) edge[->] node[noded] {\tiny\color{black} 1} node[above]{(3)[3]} node[below]{ {\color{red} 0} } (a) ;
	\draw [thick] (s) edge[->] node[noded] {\tiny\color{black} 2} node[below]{(4)[1]} node[above]{ {\color{red} 0}} (b) ;
	\draw [thick] (a) edge[->] node[noded] {\tiny\color{black} 3} node[above]{(3)[2]} node[below]{ {\color{red} 0}} (c) ;
	\draw [thick] (b) edge[->] node[noded] {\tiny\color{black} 4} node[left]{(1)[5]} node[right]{ {\color{red} 0} } (c) ;
	\draw [thick] (b) edge[->] node[noded] {\tiny\color{black} 5} node[below]{(4)[2]} node[above]{{\color{red} 0}} (t) ;
	\draw [thick] (c) edge[->] node[noded] {\tiny\color{black} 6}  node[right]{(3)[1]} node[left]{{\color{red} 0}} (t) ;
	\end{tikzpicture}

\vfill
\end{center}
\only<2>{
Hay tres cadenas de $s$ a $t$:
\begin{align*}
& s\rightarrow a\rightarrow c\rightarrow t & \text{ Coste: } 6
\\
& s\rightarrow b\rightarrow t & \text{ Coste: } 3
\\
& s\rightarrow b\rightarrow c\rightarrow t & \text{ Coste: } 7
\end{align*}
Se selecciona la de menor coste y se incrementa en 4 unidades. 
}
\only<3>{¿Cuál es la solución óptima para $\nu=3$ o $\nu=5$?}
\end{frame}

\begin{frame}{Flujo compatible a coste mínimo}
\alert{Demostración del teorema 2}

Partimos de la solución a coste mínimo para un valor de flujo $\nu$ y se encuentra una cadena incremental de $s$ a $t$ de coste mínimo.

Hay que demostrar que al incrementar el flujo a lo largo de la cadena de $\delta$ unidades, la solución es óptima para $\nu+\delta$.

Por reducción al absurdo y aplicando el teorema 1, si no lo fuera, existiría un ciclo incremental de coste negativo:
\begin{itemize}
	\item Si este ciclo NO comparte arcos con la cadena, la solucion inicial no sería óptima para $\nu$.
	\item Por tanto, el ciclo y la cadena comparten arcos:  uniéndolos se puede formar una nueva cadena incremental de menor coste, lo que es contradictorio con que la cadena sea de coste mínimo.
\end{itemize}
\end{frame}

\begin{frame}{Flujo compatible a coste mínimo}{Algoritmo}
Partiendo de la red $G=(V,U)$ y un flujo inicial $\vf$, se define el siguiente grafo asociado a las distancias: $G(\vf)=(V,U')$.

\medskip

Para cada par de nodos $a,b\in V$, se definen los siguientes arcos en $U'$, 
\begin{itemize}
	\item $(a,b)\in U'$ con peso $p_{ab}$ si $f_{ab}<c_{ab}$ y $f_{ba}=0$. 
	\item $(a,b)\in U'$ con peso $\min\{p_{ab},-p_{ba}\}$ si $f_{ab}<c_{ab}$ y $f_{ba}>0$. 
	\item $(a,b)\in U'$ con peso $-p_{ab}$ si $f_{ab}=c_{ab}$ y $f_{ba}>0$. 
	\item $(a,b)\in U'$ con peso $+\infty$ si $f_{ab}=c_{ab}$ y $f_{ba}=0$. 
\end{itemize}
Nota: si en el grafo original no existe el arco de $(i,j)$, se considera $f_{ij}=c_{ij}=0$.
\end{frame}

\begin{frame}{Flujo compatible a coste mínimo}{Algoritmo}
El problema de encontrar cadenas incrementales de coste mínimo en la red inicial se traduce en encontrar caminos de longitud mínima entre $s$ y $t$ en este nuevo grafo.

\medskip

Una vez obtenido el camino mínimo, el siguiente paso es determinar el incremento de flujo a lo largo del mismo.

\medskip

Nota: un circuito de longitud negativa en $G(\vf)$ se corresponde con un ciclo incremental de coste negativo.
\end{frame}

\begin{frame}{Flujo compatible a coste mínimo}{Algoritmo}\small
\begin{enumerate}[\mbox{P} 1] 
	\item Inicialización. Obtener un flujo compatible inicial $\vf$.
	\item Eliminación de ciclos con coste negativo.
	\begin{enumerate}
	\item Construir el grafo $G(\vf)$.
	\item Si tiene circuitos de longitud negativa, modificar el flujo y repetir el paso 2.
	\item En caso contrario, ir al paso 3.
	\end{enumerate}
	\item Aumento del valor del flujo.
	
	Si $f_0=\nu$, parar. En caso contrario, buscar en $G(\vf)$ el camino de longitud mínima de $s$ a $t$.
	\begin{enumerate}
		\item Si no existe, parar, el problema no tiene solución.
		\item En caso contrario, calcular la capacidad mínima residual de la cadena asociada al camino $c(s,t)$.
		\item Variar el flujo en los arcos del camino en $\min\{c(s,t),\nu-f_0\}$.
		\item Construir el grafo asociado al nuevo flujo e ir al paso 3.
	\end{enumerate}
\end{enumerate}
\end{frame}

\begin{frame}{Flujo compatible a coste mínimo}{Algoritmo. Ejemplo}
\only<1>{Objetivo: $\nu=5$}
\only<2>{Paso 0. $\vf = \vec{0}$}
\only<3>{Paso 1. Grafo $G(\vf)$}
\only<4>{Paso 1. Grafo $G(\vf)$. Circuito de longitud negativa (cap. res = 2)}
\only<5>{Paso 1. Se incrementa el flujo en el circuito}
\only<6>{Paso 1. Nuevo grafo $G(\vf)$}
\only<7>{Paso 1. Nuevo grafo $G(\vf)$. No tiene circuitos de longitud negativa. Ir al paso 2}
\only<8>{Paso 2. Como $f_0=0<5=\nu$, obtener el camino mínimo de $s$ a $t$}
\only<9>{Paso 2. Coste del camino $s-a-t$: 3-1=2. Capacidad residual: 3. Incremento $\min\{3,4-0\}=3$}
\only<10>{Paso 2. Se incrementa el flujo en el camino, $f_0=3$}
\only<11>{Paso 2. Como $f_0=3<5=\nu$, se actualiza el grafo}
\only<12>{Paso 2. Se obtiene el camino mínimo de $s$ a $t$}
\only<13>{Paso 2. Coste del camino: 2. Capacidad residual: 1. Incremento $\min\{1,5-3\}=1$}
\only<14>{Paso 2. Se incrementa el flujo en el camino, $f_0=4$}
\only<15>{Paso 2. Como $f_0<\nu$, se actualiza el grafo}
\only<16>{Paso 2. Se obtiene el camino mínimo de $s$ a $t$}
\only<17>{Paso 2. Coste del camino: 4. Capacidad residual: 1. Incremento $\min\{1,5-4\}=1$}
\only<18>{Paso 2. Se incrementa el flujo en el camino, $f_0=5$}
\only<19>{Paso 2. Como $f_0=\nu$, se para. La solución actual es óptima}
\begin{center}
	\begin{tikzpicture}[node_style/.style={draw,circle,minimum size=0.5cm,inner sep=1},noded/.style={fill=lightgray,circle,inner sep=0.5}]
	\node[node_style] (s) at (0,1.5) {s} ;
	\node[node_style] (a) at (3,3) {a} ;
	\node[node_style] (b) at (3,0) {b} ;
	\node[node_style] (c) at (6,0.5) {c} ;
	\node[node_style] (t) at (8,2.5) {t} ;
	\only<1>{
	\draw [thick] (s) edge[->] node[noded] {\tiny\color{black} 1} node[above]{(3)[3]}  (a) ;
	\draw [thick] (s) edge[->] node[noded] {\tiny\color{black} 2} node[below]{(2)[3]}  (b) ;
	\draw [thick] (a) edge[->] node[noded] {\tiny\color{black} 3} node[above]{(2)[-1]}  (c) ;
	\draw [thick] (a) edge[->] node[noded] {\tiny\color{black} 4} node[above]{(4)[-1]}  (t) ;
	\draw [thick] (b) edge[->] node[noded] {\tiny\color{black} 5} node[left]{(2)[-2]}  (a) ;
	\draw [thick] (c) edge[->] node[noded] {\tiny\color{black} 6} node[below]{(2)[1]} (b) ;
	\draw [thick] (c) edge[->] node[noded] {\tiny\color{black} 7} node[right]{(5)[2]}  (t) ;
	}
	\onslide<2>{
		\draw [thick] (s) edge[->]  node[above]{(3)[3]} node[below]{ {\color{red} 0} } (a) ;
		\draw [thick] (s) edge[->]  node[below]{(2)[3]} node[above]{ {\color{red} 0}} (b) ;
		\draw [thick] (a) edge[->]  node[right]{(2)[-1]} node[left]{ {\color{red} 0}} (c) ;
		\draw [thick] (a) edge[->]  node[above]{(4)[-1]} node[below]{ {\color{red} 0}} (t) ;
		\draw [thick] (b) edge[->]  node[left]{(2)[-2]} node[right]{ {\color{red} 0} } (a) ;
		\draw [thick] (c) edge[->]  node[below]{(2)[1]} node[above]{{\color{red} 0}} (b) ;
		\draw [thick] (c) edge[->]  node[right]{(5)[2]} node[left]{{\color{red} 0}} (t) ;
	}
	\only<3-4>{
		\draw [thick,color=blue] (s) edge[->]  node[above]{3}  (a) ;
		\draw [thick,color=blue] (s) edge[->]  node[below]{3}  (b) ;
		\draw [thick,color=blue] (a) edge[->]  node[above]{-1}  (t) ;
		\draw [thick,color=blue] (c) edge[->]  node[right]{2} (t) ;
		\only<3>{
			\draw [thick,color=blue] (a) edge[->]  node[right]{-1}  (c) ;
			\draw [thick,color=blue] (b) edge[->]  node[left]{-2}  (a) ;
			\draw [thick,color=blue] (c) edge[->]  node[below]{1}  (b) ;
		}
		\only<4>{
			\draw [very thick,color=red] (a) edge[->]  node[right]{-1}  (c) ;
			\draw [very thick,color=red] (b) edge[->]  node[left]{-2}  (a) ;
			\draw [very thick,color=red] (c) edge[->]  node[below]{1}  (b) ;
		}
	}
	\only<5>{
		\draw [thick] (s) edge[->]  node[above]{(3)[3]} node[below]{ {\color{red} 0} } (a) ;
		\draw [thick] (s) edge[->]  node[below]{(2)[3]} node[above]{ {\color{red} 0}} (b) ;
		\draw [thick] (a) edge[->]  node[right]{(2)[-1]} node[left]{ {\color{red} 2}} (c) ;
		\draw [thick] (a) edge[->]  node[above]{(4)[-1]} node[below]{ {\color{red} 0}} (t) ;
		\draw [thick] (b) edge[->]  node[left]{(2)[-2]} node[right]{ {\color{red} 2} } (a) ;
		\draw [thick] (c) edge[->]  node[below]{(2)[1]} node[above]{{\color{red} 2}} (b) ;
		\draw [thick] (c) edge[->]  node[right]{(5)[2]} node[left]{{\color{red} 0}} (t) ;
	}
	\only<6-7>{
		\draw [thick,color=blue] (s) edge[->]  node[above]{3}  (a) ;
		\draw [thick,color=blue] (s) edge[->]  node[below]{3}  (b) ;
		\draw [thick,color=blue] (a) edge[->]  node[above]{-1}  (t) ;
		\draw [thick,color=blue] (c) edge[->]  node[right]{2} (t) ;
		\draw [thick,color=blue] (c) edge[->]  node[right]{1}  (a) ;
		\draw [thick,color=blue] (a) edge[->]  node[left]{2}  (b) ;
		\draw [thick,color=blue] (b) edge[->]  node[below]{-1}  (c) ;
	}
	\only<8-9>{
	\draw [thick,color=blue] (s) edge[->]  node[below]{3}  (b) ;
	\draw [thick,color=blue] (c) edge[->]  node[right]{2} (t) ;
	\draw [thick,color=blue] (a) edge[->]  node[right]{-1}  (c) ;
	\draw [thick,color=blue] (b) edge[->]  node[left]{-2}  (a) ;
	\draw [thick,color=blue] (c) edge[->]  node[below]{1}  (b) ;
	\only<8>{
		\draw [thick,color=blue] (s) edge[->]  node[above]{3}  (a) ;
		\draw [thick,color=blue] (a) edge[->]  node[above]{-1}  (t) ;
	}
	\only<9>{
		\draw [very thick,color=red] (s) edge[->]  node[above]{3}  (a) ;
		\draw [very thick,color=red] (a) edge[->]  node[above]{-1}  (t) ;
	}
	}
	\only<10>{
		\draw [thick] (s) edge[->]  node[above]{(3)[3]} node[below]{ {\color{red} 3} } (a) ;
		\draw [thick] (s) edge[->]  node[below]{(2)[3]} node[above]{ {\color{red} 0}} (b) ;
		\draw [thick] (a) edge[->]  node[right]{(2)[-1]} node[left]{ {\color{red} 2}} (c) ;
		\draw [thick] (a) edge[->]  node[above]{(4)[-1]} node[below]{ {\color{red} 3}} (t) ;
		\draw [thick] (b) edge[->]  node[left]{(2)[-2]} node[right]{ {\color{red} 2} } (a) ;
		\draw [thick] (c) edge[->]  node[below]{(2)[1]} node[above]{{\color{red} 2}} (b) ;
		\draw [thick] (c) edge[->]  node[right]{(5)[2]} node[left]{{\color{red} 0}} (t) ;
	}
	\only<11-13>{
		\draw [thick,color=blue] (c) edge[->]  node[right]{2} (t) ;
		\draw [thick,color=blue] (a) edge[->]  node[left]{2}  (b) ;
		\draw [thick,color=blue] (a) edge[->]  node[above]{-3}  (s) ;
		\draw [thick,color=blue] (t) edge[->,bend right=8]  node[above]{1}  (a) ;
		\only<11>{
			\draw [thick,color=blue] (s) edge[->]  node[below]{3}  (b) ;
			\draw [thick,color=blue] (b) edge[->]  node[below]{-1}  (c) ;
			\draw [thick,color=blue] (c) edge[->]  node[right]{1}  (a) ;
			\draw [thick,color=blue] (a) edge[->,bend right=8]  node[below]{-1}  (t) ;
		}
		\only<12-13>{
			\draw [very thick,color=red] (s) edge[->]  node[below]{3}  (b) ;
			\draw [very thick,color=red] (b) edge[->]  node[below]{-1}  (c) ;
			\draw [very thick,color=red] (c) edge[->]  node[right]{1}  (a) ;
			\draw [very thick,color=red] (a) edge[->,bend right=8]  node[below]{1}  (t) ;
		}
	}
	\only<14>{
		\draw [thick] (s) edge[->]  node[above]{(3)[3]} node[below]{ {\color{red} 3} } (a) ;
		\draw [thick] (s) edge[->]  node[below]{(2)[3]} node[above]{ {\color{red} 1}} (b) ;
		\draw [thick] (a) edge[->]  node[right]{(2)[-1]} node[left]{ {\color{red} 1}} (c) ;
		\draw [thick] (a) edge[->]  node[above]{(4)[-1]} node[below]{ {\color{red} 4}} (t) ;
		\draw [thick] (b) edge[->]  node[left]{(2)[-2]} node[right]{ {\color{red} 2} } (a) ;
		\draw [thick] (c) edge[->]  node[below]{(2)[1]} node[above]{{\color{red} 1}} (b) ;
		\draw [thick] (c) edge[->]  node[right]{(5)[2]} node[left]{{\color{red} 0}} (t) ;
	}
	\only<15-17>{
	\draw [thick,color=blue] (b) edge[->,bend right=8]  node[above]{-3}  (s) ;
	\draw [thick,color=blue] (a) edge[->]  node[above]{-3}  (s) ;
	\draw [thick,color=blue] (a) edge[->]  node[left]{2}  (b) ;
	\draw [thick,color=blue] (a) edge[->,bend right=8]  node[below]{-1}  (c) ;
	\draw [thick,color=blue] (c) edge[->,bend right=8]  node[above]{1}  (a) ;
	\draw [thick,color=blue] (t) edge[->]  node[below]{1}  (a) ;
	\draw [thick,color=blue] (c) edge[->,bend right=8]  node[above]{1}  (b) ;
	\only<15>{
		\draw [thick,color=blue] (s) edge[->,bend right=8]  node[below]{3}  (b) ;
		\draw [thick,color=blue] (b) edge[->,bend right=8]  node[below]{-1}  (c) ;
		\draw [thick,color=blue] (c) edge[->]  node[right]{2} (t) ;
	}
	\only<16-17>{
		\draw [very thick,color=red] (s) edge[->,bend right=8]  node[below]{3}  (b) ;
		\draw [very thick,color=red] (b) edge[->,bend right=8]  node[below]{-1}  (c) ;
		\draw [very thick,color=red] (c) edge[->]  node[right]{2} (t) ;
	}
}
\only<18-19>{
	\draw [thick] (s) edge[->]  node[above]{(3)[3]} node[below]{ {\color{red} 3} } (a) ;
	\draw [thick] (s) edge[->]  node[below]{(2)[3]} node[above]{ {\color{red} 2}} (b) ;
	\draw [thick] (a) edge[->]  node[right]{(2)[-1]} node[left]{ {\color{red} 1}} (c) ;
	\draw [thick] (a) edge[->]  node[above]{(4)[-1]} node[below]{ {\color{red} 4}} (t) ;
	\draw [thick] (b) edge[->]  node[left]{(2)[-2]} node[right]{ {\color{red} 2} } (a) ;
	\draw [thick] (c) edge[->]  node[below]{(2)[1]} node[above]{{\color{red} 0}} (b) ;
	\draw [thick] (c) edge[->]  node[right]{(5)[2]} node[left]{{\color{red} 1}} (t) ;
}
	\end{tikzpicture}
\end{center}

\end{frame}

\section{Problema del transbordo}
\begin{frame}{Problema del transbordo}
%%
El problema consiste en determinar cómo transportar una mercancía a través de una red de capacidad limitada (con cotas $\ell_{ij}$ y $u_{ij}$ en cada arco), en la que algunos nodos ofertan mercancía y otros la demanda, de forma que este transporte se haga a coste mínimo

\begin{itemize}
	\item
	Variables: $f_{ij}$, número de unidades enviadas de $i$ a $j$,
	$\forall (i,j)\in U$.
	\item
	Costes: $p_{ij}$, coste unitario de transporte de $i$ a $j$,
	$\forall (i,j)\in U$.
	\item
	Mercancía disponibles en un nodo $i$, $i\in V$:
	\begin{itemize}
		\item $b_i>0$, oferta
		\item $b_i<0$, demanda
		\item $b_i=0$, transbordo
	\end{itemize}
\end{itemize}
Se puede asumir que el problema es equilibrado (la oferta total es igual a la demanda total).
\end{frame}

\begin{frame}{Problema del transbordo}
%%

\alert{Restricciones:}
\begin{itemize}
	\item Conservación de flujo (se extiende la definición):
	\begin{itemize}
		\item El flujo que sale del nodo $i$, $\sum_{j=1}^n f_{ij}$,
		\item menos el flujo que llega al nodo $i$, $\sum_{k=1}^n f_{ki}$,
		\item debe coincidir con el recurso disponible en el nodo $i$, $b_i$.
	\end{itemize}
	$$
	\sum_{j=1}^n f_{ij} - \sum_{k=1}^n f_{ki} = b_i, \quad i\in V
	$$
	\item Cotas: $\ell_{ij}\leq f_{ij} \leq c_{ij}$, $\forall (i,j)\in U$.
\end{itemize} 

\alert{Objetivo:}
$$\displaystyle
\min\sum_{i=1}^n\sum_{j=1}^n p_{ij}f_{ij}
$$
\end{frame}

\begin{frame}{Problema del transbordo}{Casos particulares}
\begin{itemize}\itemsep 5mm
	\item Problema de asignación
	\item Problema del transporte
	\item Problema de flujo máximo
	\item Problema de flujo compatible a coste mínimo.
	\item Problema de camino mínimo.
\end{itemize}
\end{frame}

\begin{frame}

 \frametitle{Bibliograf\'ia}

  \bibliographystyle{plain}
  \begin{thebibliography}{10}

  \bibitem{ford1956}
  Lester R. Ford.
  \newblock Network flow theory.
  \newblock {\em Paper P-923, RAND Corporation},
  1956.

  \bibitem{fordfulkerson1956}
  Lester R. Ford and Delbert R. Fulkerson.
  \newblock Maximal flow through a network.
  \newblock {\em Canadian Journal of Mathematics}, 8:399-404,
  1956.

  \end{thebibliography}

\end{frame}



{%\1% % this is the name of the PDF file for the background
\begin{frame}[plain] % the plain option removes the header from the title page, noframenumbering removes the numbering of this frame only
  \titlepage % call the title page information from above
\end{frame}}

\end{document}
